name: Fonda Lo Que Hay
address: XF27+RH7 Edificio el Colegio, C. José D. de la Obaldía, Panama City, Panamá Province, Panama
schedule: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Luis “Nacalaochul” A.", "rating": 4, "text": "Located at Casco Viejo Panamá. Diners inside or al fresco at its small central atrium. Fusion of creole Panamanian dishes, flavors, and spices with an innovating gourmet twist.
Variety of crafty cocktails, wine selection, and beers.
The tamarind margaritas are superb and local beers very good.
The Yuca tostada de atun, is like a tuna carpaccio with ceviche style onion sauce; simply excellent and delicious. The bollo preñao de pulpo. Tiny but flavorful bits of octopus inside cornmeal and the sauce is to die for.
Fried fish and seafood medlay to share. Fish always fresh and the patacones (fried plantains) fried to perfection (crispy outside and moist inside).
Great ambiance, decor, and nice bar.
Walk ins possible but reservations highly suggested. Parking may be very challenging.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-16T21:55:54.593527172Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Cedric Chen", "rating": 1, "text": "A total disappointment, easily our worst dining experience in Panama during our two-week stay.

This restaurant came highly recommended by both social media and friends who had previously visited, so expectations were high.

We arrived for lunch on Saturday with a 1:30 pm reservation and were seated promptly. We ordered two appetizers and two mains: the tuna tostada, grilled oyster, duck, and fried fish.

The tuna tostada ($18) arrived within about 10 minutes, followed by the grilled oyster roughly 20 minutes later. While both tasted fine, the value was extremely poor. For context, we’re from Toronto, and a similar tuna dish from a Michelin-starred restaurant like Akira Back costs similarly  but comes with far better quality and larger portion size. The grilled oyster was $15 and consisted of a single oyster (pictured).

The real issues were with the main courses. The duck ($35) and fried fish ($55) arrived a full hour after we sat down, at 2:30 pm. Only after I asked where our entree's were, did we receive an explaination (no apology) that the kitchen had mistakenly sent our dishes to another table and had to remake them. Completely unacceptable at this price point.

Unfortunately, the food did not redeem the experience. The duck was inedible. Extremely fatty, tough,and impossible to chew (filled with Sinew /muscle fiber), with soggy skin. The fried fish itself was fine, though the curry shrimp that accompanied it was actually the highlight. The crab, however, was completely tasteless and watery.

Our bill came to $125 for two people before tip (which I still left 10%) . We left most of the duck untouched and departed at 2:46 pm (receipt included).

Save your time and money and dine elsewhere. Don’t be swayed by the online hype.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-20T22:56:57.623393337Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Moe Mandour", "rating": 4, "text": "Lovely experience at a place recommended by a lot of friends. Honestly, it wasn’t my top two spots in Panama, but the steak was our favorite, so tender and delicious. The salads were a bit salty and acidic, even though they said no salt is added. The service was outstanding, and the staff were clearly helpful and friendly. Overall, it really comes down to personal taste, so it’s worth trying for yourself. Just make sure to reserve at least 2–3 nights in advance!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-15T16:28:32.877773446Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}, {"author": "AJM", "rating": 5, "text": "The food was excellent, without a doubt, the tuna carpaccio was unlike anything I had tried before, really bright, fresh ans crunchy, my favorite. The chicken also was spot on the mash too. I felt the staff could have been more attentive, it took a while for them to respond to a simple request on more than one occasion. But the food is legit, so id go back.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-03T20:31:21.436335758Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Sherry Zhang", "rating": 5, "text": "The tuna entree was amazing, we also ordered sexy clams and grilled octopus, both super flavorsome, octopus was very tender. The yellow color dish is some sort of potato, sauce is similar to thai flavor. Service was attentive and environment was pleasant. A 10 out of 10 experience.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-21T18:17:18.105973497Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}]

Service was fast, waitress was very attentive and gave recommendations, and was quick to get new drinks during Happy Hour.  Food came out quickly and hot and were all as described in the menu.
I would recommend making a reservation prior if you go during busy times as it is somewhat small, however they do have some open tables that people were able to walk in and sit down at.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-20T15:05:57.220889068Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "António Vila Maior", "rating": 5, "text": "We arrived close to 11h15pm and were extremely well attended to.
The food was very good and well prepared, as were the cocktails.

We ordered the BBQ Burger (which was exactly what I was craving at that hour, and it was excellent), and we also had a ceviche that was very good. The portions of fries (which accompanied the burger) and the ceviche itself were a bit small.

The service was very attentive, with the staff being patient while we took our time choosing the main course. As for the atmosphere, I can’t say much since there weren’t many tables occupied due to the time we arrived. It’s a piquito space with a warm and welcoming environment appeal.  The staff were super attentive and even told us of other places to go.

Our server Moses was very helpful.
The happy hour drinks were tasty.  I had a Long Island tea -something I haven’t had in many moons (tomorrow’s the blue moon don’t miss it-May30).

I had the hummus and beef with pita bread.   YES MORE PLZ!
We are here for my lil sis and big bro anniversary so overall we look for experiences- no matter how big or small.  gracias 🙏
Disclaimer: hummus and beef not included I. Images.  Images are of the ribs.  Sorry I forgot. name: Mahalo Cocina y Jardin
address: Las Clementinas Hotel, C. 11 Este, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 7:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Tuesday: 7:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 7:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 7:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Friday: 7:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Saturday: 7:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Sunday: 7:30 AM – 10:30 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Génesis Pineda", "rating": 5, "text": "We had an amazing experience at Mahalo Kitchen! We ordered the huevos al montuno, the classic breakfast, and the yogurt bowl, everything was absolutely delicious. The flavors were fresh and well-balanced, and you can really tell they use quality ingredients.

The portions were generous and very satisfying without being overwhelming. On top of that, the service was excellent, friendly, attentive, and welcoming from start to finish.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-04T04:00:37.314041766Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Lena Schenk", "rating": 5, "text": "We went here for dinner the other night, and really loved it. The service was amazing, and the setting cute. We sat by the bar, which had nice vibes, but the courtyard looked lovely too!

Veggie burger and gnocchi were solid, but our favourite dish was the broccolini! The sauce was incredible. Also, we did not have any cocktails but the bar tender was mixing up some really good looking ones!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-04T19:17:28.450622881Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Peter Kapshuk", "rating": 5, "text": "This is a wonderful restaurant in the historic district of Panamá City that has a nice selection of breakfast, brunch, lunch and dinner options including many vegetarian dishes. I had lunch here and they featured lunch specials for a set price that included a choice of side dish and beverage. You are also able to choose items from their regular menu. I ordered a fish ceviche and grilled carrot dish as starters and then the egg salad sandwich with a green salad (an item that was listed on the lunch specials menu). I had a fresh green juice and hot chocolate to drink and banana cheesecake for dessert. All the items were fresh, flavourful and generous in portion size. My server Madeleyn was courteous, professional and spoke fluent English. The ambiance was calm, casual and relaxed. The unisex washroom was well maintained. This place has a counter seating available adjacent to the bar. I highly recommend this restaurant to those that enjoy delicious comfort food that includes many healthy options served by attentive staff in a comfortable environment.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-19T23:50:26.194873667Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "TravelReviews", "rating": 5, "text": "Phenomenal! 5-stars all across. I loved my Shrimp Rustico and my partner's gnocchi was outstanding. The indoor dining seemed very nice and comfy, but a little chilly from AC... So the waiter asked if we wanted the garden area - what a great outdoor setting! I wish I had taken more pics, but was busy devouring shrimp 😁😍", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-16T20:13:10.004260480Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Eloise Eaton", "rating": 5, "text": "My tastes are simple. A secret garden with an inside-outside feel. Cute tables, a canopy of Edison bulbs strung overhead. OK, so I also want great food and friendly service. Like I said, I don’t ask for much.

It’s always a high-stress (OK, so a slight exaggeration) moment walking through the door. Who will greet you? How? Will they love me enough to give me an awesome table? TBH, if I sit down and don’t like it because I didn’t pass whatever kind of secret measurement you have, then I’m outta there.

I fell in love with Mahalo Cocina y Jardin immediately.

The happy, friendly, I-will-make-sure-you-are-well-looked-after greeter gave me a great table in their magical garden. Please, please, please let the food be good.

I ordered the cauliflower to start. The plan was to share it with Husband but I decided the wise thing to do would be to divorce him so I could have the lot. I have no idea what the sauce was but I now I need to eat it everyday.
When Husband and I rekindled our love he ordered the burger. It oozed with all the right things, specifically bacon jam. I had the Asian chicken bowl and silently coveted his bacon jam. But not fo long. My chicken was quite bland but bacon jam fixes everything. That and the one fry I was allowed.

We were so well looked after I forgave the chicken and would happily go back and live on the cauliflower.

A cute and magical place and I defy anyone to say otherwise.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-11T23:19:57.947719149Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}]

Our appetizer - Cobia ceviche was very good and the best of our 3 dishes. My husband's trout was flavorful but overcooked and dry. My pork was also overdone despite asking for it to be pink and it was also lukewarm and dry. [{"author": "BeDee E", "rating": 4, "text": "This establishment has a beautiful view of the city. The overall customer service was good although the turnaround time was little bit slow.

The food was delicious and the restaurant surprised me with a little cake for my birthday celebration.  I enjoyed the music and the overall ambiance of the restaurant was pleasant.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-25T01:10:27.819536961Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Edward L Carter", "rating": 4, "text": "Armando was our waiter. He said he didn’t speak English, but he did very well explaining everything in English the food was OK. The first round of drink for their signature. Drinks were OK but the presentation was nice. This restaurant is all about the ambience, the view and the experience my wife and I had a good time. No complaints. I ordered the lemongrass fish with baked potato, and my wife ordered the steak for appetizer. We had the empanadas they were good but the chimichurri sauce could use some work.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-10T15:13:16.216240571Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Bryan Stanley", "rating": 3, "text": "Although very well located near Casco Viejo, the access streets are dimly lit and feel abandoned making the entrance an uncomfortable experience.

The restaurant is a rooftop experience with open air terrace and a smaller indoor space which has an African theme/decor that is very basic.

The atmosphere is accompanied by a DJ who seemed to play a constant beat that was tiring after a while.

The menu was limited, but what we ordered was very tasty.

We had Ceviche, hot rock beef strips, rib eye and a white fish grilled with lemon sauce.

Unfortunately the fish was nicely charred on the outside but not sufficiently cooked on the inside.

We ordered Vodka and Soda and Paloma’s.

But received a margarita instead. Twice.

So we gave up trying to order a Paloma grapefruit juice and tequila).

We also ordered a desert which was never provided even though the server acknowledged our order. But it was not included in the final bill.

We dined from 8-11. At 8 the restaurant was quite empty but as we left around 11, the restaurant began to fill up - which surprised us as we were there on a Sunday night.

Bottom line - festive atmosphere but over priced for the food and service quality.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-10T12:07:09.786623891Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "Shawny B", "rating": 5, "text": "You can’t ask for a better roof top dining experience. You’ll love it.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-06T08:30:42.472372922Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "M Hooks", "rating": 2, "text": "Beautiful atmosphere and nice staff but my chicken entree was inedible and when I pointed it out to the staff I was told it was safe to eat because it had been sous vide cooked. I was baffled because the chicken was clearly pink and they didn't even reduce the bill. Also I wouldn't recommend the mushroom ravioli, it had the texture of baby food. My wife had a great steak but I can't get passed the raw chicken.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-21T03:05:15.298446873Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}]

name: Cafe Coca Cola
address: XF36+9JQ, Av. Central España, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Panama George", "rating": 5, "text": "This place is the closest thing to an American diner that you will find anywhere in the vicinity. Not exactly the same but close enough due to the amount of traditional fare such as pancakes, omelets, French toast, tartar sauce, steaks and salads. No surprise you will hear wall-to-wall English being spoken.

Of course it also includes classic Panamanian dishes such as breaded sea bass, sancocho soup, fried dough bread, and corn tortillas. Overall, this is a convenient place because it combines a myriad of my favorite Panamanian and American dishes. I have tried the pancakes, vegetable omelet, pork sandwich, and found them to be okay.

Tourists may find the familiarity to a diner to be comforting. Take the opportunity to try Panamanian food here in case hesitancy from a lack of Spanish language skills may make you hesitant to enter an actual Creole fonda.

In the first page of the menu you can read about the history. Apparently it is the first ever cafe in the country since 1875. It was granted permission from the Coca-Cola company to use its name - the only cafe in the world to be given that privilege.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-20T03:09:39.758495417Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "José Chang Leung", "rating": 5, "text": "A Cafe opened since 1875. This is a must go place if you need good Panamanian food in Panama. It is like going to Casa Botín in Madrid. Price are very good and quality are good like their early days. You can try the breakfast, lunch and dinner. Definitely the best in Casco Antiguo.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-08T17:22:19.275384911Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Kristina", "rating": 5, "text": "Café Coca-Cola has such a cozy, nostalgic charm. The atmosphere is welcoming, the food is simple and delicious, and the service makes you feel right at home. It’s the perfect place to enjoy a relaxed meal and soak in a bit of Panama’s history.  Paid $26 for steak with veggies and fries, cubana sandwich, cheesecake and two drinks. Definitely worth a visit!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-12T02:49:58.469351539Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Sinem Alparslan", "rating": 4, "text": "If I had only ordered shrimp, I would definitely give this place five stars, but after the meat I just had, I'm a bit disappointed because it was very tough. Yes, it was tasty, but it was excessively tough. But atmosphere is super nice
Prices are super affordable
Look at this - 3 Portion and this is the price", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-25T18:54:43.957588938Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Steven", "rating": 4, "text": "Café Coca Cola – Panama City
Great café right in the heart of Casco Viejo!
Lovely atmosphere, tasty drinks, and a solid breakfast. The coffee was rich and flavorful, the staff friendly, and the location perfect for recharging after a long stroll through the historic district. Prices are reasonable, and you really get a relaxed, local vibe. Definitely worth a visit!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-09T13:51:47.256797409Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}]

reviews_json:
[{"author": "Keisha Unger-Times", "rating": 5, "text": "This was the 3rd and last place we went to on our night out. We had so much fun. It was crowded when we first arrived (sometime after midnight) but the crowd thinned as the night went on.

Music spanned Spanish pop to hip-hop.  We had a blast. We danced and sometimes the servers joined in. Service was quick and friendly

We danced and had a blast. We had a variety of drinks purchased by our group however I didnt personally pay so I cant speak to the costs per drink.

Most important was the view! Sama is a rooftop bar with an amazing view of the city. From one side you're overlooking Casco Antiguo and in the background is the modern city. Amazing view", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-10T03:13:29.942863886Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Shar Dorsey", "rating": 4, "text": "Great views and service. My espresso martini was also made to perfection. The building itself is the reason I had to remove a star. It was hard locating and could use some renovations.

Overall good experience though and would recommend.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-06-01T14:49:54.884296758Z", "relative_time": "a week ago"}, {"author": "Martha Lopez", "rating": 5, "text": "Sana Sky Lounge in Casco Viejo is an absolute must! This beautiful rooftop perfectly captures the vibrant soul of Panama. From the moment we arrived, the atmosphere was incredible—stunning views, great energy, and the perfect mix of locals and visitors.
A very special shout-out to Carlos, who took exceptional care of us all night. His service was warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. To make the experience even better, he played salsa music that truly set the mood and gave us those authentic Panama vibes. Dancing, great drinks, amazing music, and a magical rooftop setting—it doesn’t get better than this.
If you’re looking for a place with great service, fantastic music, and unforgettable rooftop vibes in Casco Viejo, Sana Sky Lounge is the place to go. We can’t wait to come back!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-22T23:55:21.542178495Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Melinda Erdélyi", "rating": 4, "text": "We went to this rooftop bar in the Old Town mainly for the view, and because there weren’t many places open before 4 p.m. It turned out to be a great choice.

The view is stunning, with a beautiful panorama over the city. Cocktails were very good, and the service was friendly and attentive.

It started raining while we were there, but we could easily move into the covered area and still enjoy the atmosphere and the scenery.

A lovely rooftop spot with great views and a relaxed vibe — definitely worth a visit.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-18T11:06:54.597464151Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Julio Herrera", "rating": 5, "text": "Such an amazing place. I have come here many times since moving here. At this point and it has proven to be the best Rooftop bar in the city. Always great service great drinks and amazing food. I love their view. name: Central Hotel Panamá Casco Viejo
address: Plaza de la Independencia, C. 5a Este, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: hotel, bar_and_grill, swimming_pool, bar, spa, sports_activity_location, lodging, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment
schedule: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Sampath Srinath", "rating": 5, "text": "It’s so much fun staying in this UNESCO heritage area, its refurbished old town with some old structures still intact. This hotel is apparently the first one in the city and is lovely. Room was a bit small but adequate, and luxurious. They have live music even for breakfast! There are dozens of eateries and souvenir shops within a few blocks! Had a lovely stay.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-05T02:47:17.139825687Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Melinda Erdélyi", "rating": 5, "text": "Central Hotel Panamá Casco Viejo is truly one of Panama’s landmark hotels — it was originally built in 1874, making it the first hotel in Panama and nearly 150 years old.

The building is stunning and steeped in history, located right on Plaza de la Independencia in the heart of Casco Viejo, Panama City’s UNESCO World Heritage historic district.

We arrived in the evening and immediately fell in love with the old-town atmosphere around the hotel — cobblestone streets, live music, and a genuine sense of place. Inside, the ambiance reflects both its heritage and thoughtful renovations, with rich architectural details and character.

The rooms were spacious, well equipped, and comfortable, with everything you need for a relaxing stay. Breakfast was a rich buffet with plenty of options, easily one of the best breakfasts we had on our trip.

There’s live music around the entrance and in the restaurant areas, which adds beautifully to the charm and feels very authentic to the setting.

Everything about this hotel — the historic building, the location in Casco Viejo, the atmosphere, and the quality of the experience — felt like a true five-star stay and a perfect introduction to Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-18T10:57:12.328340549Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Waikit Lau", "rating": 1, "text": "Beautiful hotel. WiFi does not work on the floor we are on (4th floor) and no one is here to fix even after 2 days of feedback to the front desk. The vibe is staff isnt responsive to such 1st world problem. I live in the Caribbean so I’m well versed on island time and culture. This isn’t that, it’s worse.
Oh and the thermostat is essentially a decorative item in the room. Better and cheaper options elsewhere.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-15T16:47:12.629952448Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Pat Droll", "rating": 5, "text": "We were very pleased with our stay at the Central hotel. The service from the front desk, concierge, musicians, restaurant staff was excellent. The breakfast options were plentiful and the food very good. The restaurant food was also excellent. The room (junior suite) bed was very comfortable and clean. My only complaint, which is common amongst most hotels, is the wifi can be slow which is annoying. The shower was also small since it was a later addition but overall the bathroom was large and nice. Water pressure was excellent and the hot water was steady without any fluctuation.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-20T02:18:08.120895975Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Ayesha Khan", "rating": 2, "text": "Very Expensive. Do not get a balcony room that faces the plaza if you want to sleep. Noise 24/7. Evening reaturant and bartenders WONDERFUL. BUT, the day staff need to speak better English and be more attentive. I waited 15 minutes for a glass of water, only 1 other table !!. I moved towards the front lobby and asked to get a drink.. 30 minutes later, NOTHING. Like I said, staff not attentive. My advice - DO BETTER.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-28T01:37:15.851744772Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}]

name: Kaandela Restaurant
address: Casa 3, Amarla Casco Viejo Boutique Hotel, Av. Central España 24, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
reviews_json:
[{"author": "onceuponatimemonii", "rating": 5, "text": "Kaandela Restaurant is an absolute gem in historic downtown Panama! Located in the heart of the city just minutes from the cathedral, the restaurant is surrounded by stunning architecture and breathtaking views that create an unforgettable atmosphere.

A special thank you to Luis Afu, the restaurant manager, who provided extensive and thoughtful details about food preparation and perfect wine pairing options. His knowledge and passion truly elevated the entire dining experience.

The staff is fantastic — welcoming, attentive, and incredibly knowledgeable. You can genuinely feel their passion for hospitality from the moment you arrive.

Chef Jose Aparicio even came out, and we had the opportunity to meet and personally thank him. He is such a young and talented chef with a true passion for the art of cooking. From the rich flavors to the beautiful plating, every dish is presented like art and tastes just as incredible. I can’t emphasize enough how delicious the meal was.

I will definitely be back to continue experiencing the food, culture, and vibrant energy this place brings. Highly recommend!

P.S. If you’re lucky, you may even be able to book a stay in this exclusive location — which makes the experience even more special.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-11T04:24:52.990209844Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "The traveler", "rating": 5, "text": "Everything about Kaandela was amazing from the moment we walked in. The team greeted us with such warmth and friendliness; they communicated with us as if we were old friends coming back for a visit, which made us feel instantly welcome.
​The food experience was outstanding. We started with the Lamb Kofta and the Tongue BBQ dish—both were incredible. The open kitchen bar is a highlight; sitting there lets you watch the magic happen and chat with the chef and the team, which adds so much to the atmosphere.
​The desserts were so beautiful that we actually returned the very next day just to have the Guava pastry with cashew ice cream again. This is a must-visit spot and definitely one of the best dining experiences in the city", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-28T05:08:18.605527203Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Julio Herrera", "rating": 5, "text": "This restaurant was quaint but very nice. Be sure to have a reservation. The service was great and the food was amazing! I loved the Duck dish with risotto. We had a couple of cocktails and they were excellent. Great attention to detail. I would come back.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-26T01:47:10.222610569Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "jeanellia garcia", "rating": 5, "text": "This restaurant was nothing short of amazing. We had the 6 course meal from the central region of Panama. The fish, pork belly and their take on the “arroz con guandu” that adds a coconut curry is to die for. To top it off, the dessert “buscando guayaba” simply melts in your mouth and all the flavors from the guava and vanilla are left behind.
We enjoyed everything. For your visit, make sure to book the bar area. I made the mistake of booking regular seating and it wasn’t as fun as it could have been. The bar lets you see the kitchen and can be more interactive.
Drinks were also very good. Great service and very attentive, even when the courses came a little later than expected.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-22T01:07:57.106496105Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Desiree Bergquist", "rating": 5, "text": "A truly exceptional experience. Staff were very attentive and engaged. It was a joy to dine at the bar and watch the chef and sous chefs approach every plate with intention. We highly recommend this restaurant. It is small, reservations recommended. Jaime was a most excellent host/server.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-14T02:01:55.766832039Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}]

name: Casa Catedral | Restaurante
address: C. 5a Oeste, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: restaurant, museum, bar, food, point_of_interest, establishment
schedule: Monday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 10:00 PM
reviews_json: [{"author": "Patricia Ng", "rating": 2, "text": "I spent 19 days in Panama, normally I’m too lazy to write reviews, but I’m honestly abit pissed how I was treated from start to end, came across this restaurant on Day 1, but didn’t come until my very last day on day 19th on my last meal of my Panama visit, arrived at 830pm without reservation, then told it’s full, come back at 930pm will have a table for me. As their reviews photos and even the moment when I walked in with the live band on, it all seemed great, so I decided to wait, went away for a drink elsewhere and returned at 930pm. When I sat down at another bar, I found their WhatsApp, I sent a message to ask if they have a table for me whenever if before 930pm I will come by right away, but no one bother replying. Then when I return, a male staff somehow was by the front door as I arrived, at first he look like he’s welcoming and opening the door for me, but there’s an old lady walking out, I let her come out first before entering, that’s when this staff shut the door and didn’t let me enter and said, do you have reservation? I already saw 4 empty tables at the front door, I said no but your receptionist lady told me to come back at 930pm, then he refused to let me in, and said no reservation no entry. So I was abit shocked at how rude he was, as I didn’t see the same lady at the reception but spotted her behind the bar, I pointed and said that’s the lady told me to come back at 930pm then he let me in finally and said fine but his tone was so unwelcoming. To be treated like that to start with, I think many people would have walked away. End of the day, they are not the only restaurant in town, and plenty of great restaurants in the area, and they are not the cheap ones either. By time I arrived, the place was 70% empty, they were more in a hurry to close than wanting more customers. Even they said they close at 11pm, I ordered a starter and a main, the starter steak tacos arrived super fast & taste excellent, I have to be fair, food was amazing even tho for 3 tacos $16 it’s abit pricey as starter, then I had the octopus potato mash, it’s the best octopus I had in 19 days (consider I had 6 times in different places). However, what shocked me was after my starter finished they just collected my empty plate, then the lady staff came with the bill and the credit card machine, I thought what the?! My main hasn’t even arrived, it’s 956pm, I didn’t ask for the bill, without explaining they are trying to close the till or anything, insisting I pay the bill right away, then asked if I want to tip 10/20/30%? You seriously think first the door unwelcoming moment then this demand I pay the bill straight after my starter deserves a tip? You must be joking. I hate to act blunt, but I simply don’t think I received any good service at all, so I said no tip, then obviously I received a crap face expression, when I leave, they didn’t even say a word or thank me or say bye. What a way to end my last day and my 19 days Panama trip with the most unfriendly experience. Worst moment I’ve got in 19 days. Everywhere I’ve only met nice friendly people. What ashame! And for a starter, main course and a glass of wine (it’s poured very small glass too smaller than usual) came to $59.92. It isn’t a cheap meal. Consider expensive-ish in Panama for 1 person. I’m being fair, the food were amazing, however service & how I was treated was absolutely poor. Because of that, it’s the service had dragged down the overall  rating. If service was good I would rate this place a 4*. Overpriced, overrated, but great food minus terrible service.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-16T03:49:33.910301578Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Kevin Murphy", "rating": 5, "text": "The food was very good here! I had the catch of the day, mahi mahi, the flavors were excellent. Service was great too! It was a little quiet though, I went on a Thursday night and there was only one other table.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-17T18:31:17.882622061Z", "relative_time": "4 weeks ago"}, {"author": "Marigold 7", "rating": 5, "text": "After a long trip crossing the continents from north to south, we enjoyed a delicious dinner at this fancy restaurant. Cozy, vivid atmosphere, live music, very entartaining evening!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-22T01:39:00.602211143Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Akash Singh", "rating": 5, "text": "Great spot with some classic cars you wouldn’t expect to see. Staff is friendly and encourages you to walk around and take pictures. Drinks were fantastic!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-09-29T01:16:36.899587753Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}, {"author": "Bridgett Lerma (BridgettL)", "rating": 5, "text": "Great atmospheric restaurant close to Panama Canal Museum in old town Panama City. Really enjoyed having lunch here , and experiencing the cool vibe, interesting architecture, great service,  and old restored cars inside. Very eclectic and unique eatery offering a variety of sandwiches, salads, entrees and appetizers. They also offer a nice drink menu and wine selection.\nI had the ceviche with plantain chips which were delicious. Our server went out of his way to accommodate us and explain the history of the old restored building it occupies.\nWould highly recommend to others, and will definitely go back.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-09-14T02:15:13.264485322Z", "relative_time": "9 months ago"}]

name: Aya la Vida Restaurante | Comida Panameña Panamá
address: Avenida A, C. 12 Oeste, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 5:00 – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:00 – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 5:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Friday: 1:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 1:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Nora Naatz", "rating": 5, "text": "Great place for live music, good Panamanian cuisine and suitable for larger groups.  I recommend to make a reservation ( especially on the weekends). We came without and the manager went out of his way to accommodate us. We ended up sitting on a comfortable table a bit out of the way and maybe that’s why the waiters forgot about us. We had to wait over an hour for our food and the complementary empanadas that were send to appease the wait time were later on the bill. But I believe it was an honest mistake. If we would have made a reservation we would have had a very good experience. The food was very good and the staff was nice. The decor of the restaurant was vibrant and tropical and I particularly  loved the “tree like decor “ above the bar.  Overall a great place and I will be back to try more of their yummy food.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-27T12:19:16.344326459Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Stephen McBride", "rating": 5, "text": "What a great experience Aya la Vida was!

We came on the recommendation of friends and were so glad we did!  The restaurant was spacious with many different sized tables for groups of every size.

They had live music the night we went, which was such a fun experience to be a part of.

The food was delicious - traditional Panamanian. Don't be turned off (or confused) by some of the dish names - they did literal translations for a few.  We got the "Volcano Old Clothes Meat" - in Spanish it's "Ropa Vieja" - which is a traditional slow cooked beef.

Drinks were good, too, and all was reasonably priced.  I'd recommend a reservation, especially if you're more than 4.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-23T15:19:24.412107311Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Arina keshavarz", "rating": 2, "text": "the lady at the reception was quite rude and kept pushing us to tip more even though we were quite unsatisfied our overall experience.

we happened to be wearing casual clothes and were purposely seated at the very back behind a wall next to the bathroom and the kitchen even though the restaurant was almost EMPTY. there was live music and we couldn’t see a thing because we were hidden away behind the wall.

the food was mediocre and the rice we ordered was extremely undercooked. considering the quality pretty overpriced…", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-07T01:38:54.290594747Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Freddy Bereciartu", "rating": 5, "text": "We went to Aya La Vida as a group of four and from start to finish it was simply outstanding.

We ordered three appetizers, three main dishes, and three desserts — and every single plate was excellent. Not one weak point. Everything we tried was full of flavor, beautifully presented, and clearly made with pride. But what made the entire experience next level was our server, Sonny.

Sonny was absolutely amazing. Friendly, happy, professional, and incredibly efficient. Every dish we ordered was his recommendation — and he nailed every single one. That kind of confidence and knowledge only comes from someone who truly understands and believes in what they’re serving.

He prepared the steak tartare right at the table, explaining everything and engaging with all of us. Then he came back for dessert and did the rum flambé tableside — fire, presentation, energy — the whole experience. It’s that extra effort, that extra pride in service, that makes you genuinely happy to leave a 20–30% tip without even thinking about it.

When you find a place with great food and service that is genuinely warm and kind, it changes the entire night. You can feel that the manager, the kitchen, and the ownership are doing something special. They are showcasing quality Panamanian cuisine in a way that makes both locals and tourists proud.

Live music, dancing on Thursdays, great vibe, amazing hospitality — this place has it all.

Huge congratulations to the team at Aya La Vida, and especially to Sonny. We’ll definitely be back.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-07T03:56:23.194243357Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Halide Vera", "rating": 5, "text": "We went to Aya la Vida with a big group. They hosted us super nice. Staff were really friendly and kind. Food was very delicious. They had also a live band playing good music. 5/5!!!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-03T14:03:06.650862593Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}]

name: En La Fonda, Panamá
address: Av. Eloy Alfaro, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Floyd Spencer", "rating": 5, "text": "If you want an authentic Panamanian food and cultural ezperience, En La Fonda is thebplace to go!

Just inside the door and there's the energy of a Diablo Rojo! Okay, the front part of one, anyway. We had a group of 10 and it was cramped, but the atmosphere made up for that. There IS plenty of room, but while we made a reservation, they were not prepared for us and we ended up in the main travel zone for all the waiters.  One of our party was in a wheelchair and they accomodated us well. Just wish they would have taken the reservation seriously.

Food was good; authentic Panamnian food. Drinks were plentiful and beautifully presented.

The service was good and personable.

The show was incredible!  That alone is worth the trip! The dancers were authentic as were the musicians.

Other than the reservation issue, I would still highly recommend.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-28T02:56:57.351202213Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Arg B", "rating": 3, "text": "Food was ok. Service horrible! They took forever to served us! And no one approached to let us know what was going on or apologize for the long wait. The restaurant was not that busy when we got there. Atmosphere was good we liked the performance.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-14T22:45:02.251234192Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Maiker Suazo", "rating": 5, "text": "Great restaurant if you’re looking for local food. The wait may take a bit since is always full but it’s worth the wait. Food is delicious and price is very affordable. Would definitely come back here.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-10T03:07:07.789032546Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Farhan M.", "rating": 5, "text": "Fantastic Experience at En la Fonda Panama!
Had dinner here and absolutely loved it!

The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with a perfect mix of local charm and modern comfort. The food was outstanding- full of authentic Panamanian flavor, beautifully presented, and clearly made with care. Every dish felt fresh and perfectly seasoned.

The staff were friendly, attentive, and made us feel right at home from the moment we walked in. Whether you’re a local or just visiting, En la Fonda Panama is definitely a must-try spot for a true taste of Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-10T09:55:12.304179287Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "YoSoyBallito", "rating": 5, "text": "This place is so cool! It truly feels like a real Panamanian fonda or horón — lively, authentic, and full of local flavor. The atmosphere captures the true spirit of Panama, with music, décor, and energy that make you feel right at home.

The food is authentic, traditional Panamanian cuisine, prepared with that homemade touch that’s hard to find these days. From hearty dishes to tasty appetizers, everything is flavorful and beautifully presented. They also offer a great variety of drinks — from cocktails made with Seco Herrerano, Panama’s famous national spirit, to local beers and refreshing mixes that perfectly complement the food.

The service is outstanding! Everyone is friendly, attentive, and clearly proud of what they do, which makes the whole experience even better. Whether you’re a local or just visiting, La Fonda Panamá is the perfect spot to enjoy real Panamanian food and culture.

If you’re anywhere near Casco Antiguo, don’t miss it — it’s a must-visit for an authentic taste of Panama!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-14T22:46:44.547324765Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}]

name: CASABLANCA
address: Plaza Simón Bolívar, Calle 4a Este, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Melissa Doucette", "rating": 5, "text": "Food was delicious and beautiful. Servings were quite big. Atmosphere was comfortable and classy. Service was friendly and prompt.

The ceviche was some of the best I've had, highly recommend.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-06T03:10:37.596573064Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Phil", "rating": 5, "text": "Great attention to detail. Friendly and welcoming staff. The drinks are amazing and the bartender even explained how each one related to Panama. Will definitely be back!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-28T17:41:31.474489204Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "J W", "rating": 5, "text": "OMG. I’m in Heaven. A GIN Bar with ceviches. Not just gin, but 27 different gins. The only other place I have been to with more gins is where gin came from, Great Britain (a pub with 60 different gins the best being Whitney Neal original.) Caracruda is absolutely fantastic. From the small, intimate, welcoming piano bar music to the melt in your mouth fish mango ceviche to the hands down best grilled sea bass from the owners’ restaurant next door, Restaurante Casablanca, you will be in Heaven too. The service was impeccable. The atmosphere perfect. I’m coming back again before I leave Panama City.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-20T00:00:28.887640271Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "Bryan Stanley", "rating": 4, "text": "Great Location and atmosphere in the heart of Casco Viejo in front of Plaza Bolivar.

The restaurant and hotel is a meticulously preserved historic building with charm and friendly staff.

I wished I had more time.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-30T02:38:26.585749823Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}]

name: La Tapa Del Coco
address: Calle 68 Este, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: caribbean_restaurant, latin_american_restaurant, restaurant, point_of_interest, food, establishment
schedule: Monday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 10:30 PM
reviews_json: [{"author": "Brittany Poon-King", "rating": 5, "text": "One of the best meals we've ever had. Staff were so friendly and attentive, we were very grateful they spoke English. Wonderful atmosphere. All dishes we had were so incredibly flavourful and had wonderful textures playing together. We had the smoked pulled pork patty, cod fritters with the berry sauce, Calypso burger, one pot Colonense, pear mamallena dessert, lemonade with cane honey and pineapple refresher.\nSo glad we were able to get a reservation, highly recommend. We will return any chance we get to be in Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-27T22:04:59.244702109Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Issa Vybe", "rating": 5, "text": "We loved everything about this place. The owner introduced himself and greeted our very large party. Everyone’s food was excellent!!!! I highly recommend the half chicken, cod fish patties and fritters. Will highly recommend this place and would come back. Good food to good drinks. They did great with accommodating our over 20 people party! Food arrived hot and fresh.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-30T23:39:38.795895816Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Leeza Byers", "rating": 5, "text": "La Tapa Del Coco was absolutely one of the best restaurants in San Francisco, Panama area. The service here is impeccable - from the greeter to the waiters/waitresses. They were all extremely attentive even though it was a busy night - packed with folks from the inside to the outdoor seating area. We were celebrating our 16 year Anniversary and were delighted that we chose this place. The food is top tier here. We had the Whole Sea Bass fish with Coconut Rice and peas, Maduros (sweet plaintains) and cole slaw. We also tried the Cod fritters (dipped in their blackberry/habenero sauce) which were delectable as well. The Coconut fish soup and the pickled pig feet/pig tails were an exotic treat and did not disappoint. The coconut lime drink and the Sabril with ginger pair well with any of the dishes. They also have a nice bar for those who enjoy libations. We are definitely putting La Tapa Del Coco on our list of \"must-haves\" when in Panama!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-28T07:08:54.241799924Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Danielle Fryar", "rating": 5, "text": "It was our first time in Panama and a friend recommended that we go here.  The food was sooooo good.  We had the patties, both beef and pork.  I preferred the beef, only because I'm not a huge fan of BBQ sauce.  The pork tasted like it was made with BBQ sauce.  If it was more savory, I would probably enjoyed it more. We also had the ceviche, the clams, and finished with a pear mamallena. The ceviche was fresh and flavorful with little surprise fried squid on top, served with plantain chips. The clams were served with a coconut curry sauce.  The mamallena was a surprise I didn't know I needed.  I likened it to bread pudding in pie form but I'm not a huge fan of bread pudding, but definitely a fan of this.  They paired it with vanilla ice cream and a crunchy crumble.  It was my favorite part of the meal.  All of the flavors went well together and the crunchy crumble added a slightly salty texture.  It was perfect.  If we lived there, I'm sure I would eat here at least once a week.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-12T11:46:51.818102402Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Louis Arighi", "rating": 5, "text": "My wife and I had lunch here the last day of our trip, and we’re so glad we didn’t miss it! So many interesting options on the menu, we got the “sowse” (slow cooked pork knuckles and feet), and the ox tail stew, and both were delicious. The Bon Experience was the special desert, and was unusual but very good. The cocktails were also excellent. The staff was great, even though my Spanish is not so great, and the owner, Isaac, was sitting next to us for a little while, and was very gracious. Will definitely eat here again if I am back in Panama City!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-07T05:06:16.534244689Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}]

name: Riande Urban Hotel
address: XFPF+CPP, C. Eusebio A. Morales, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: hotel, beer_garden, bar, clothing_store, store, lodging, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment
schedule: Monday: 12:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 AM – 12:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "An Nen", "rating": 3, "text": "I can't say it was terrible, but it wasn't great either. We stayed at this hotel for 7 nights.

When we arrived and checked in, there was no drinking water, so we had to go to the restaurant and buy water for 2$ each bottle. At the 4th day I got really tired of the same breakfast. I wouldn't say that the buffet was huge, but it was the same thing every day. We also had to wait in line for breakfast few times because the breakfast area itself is quite small, and we had to wait 10-20 mins. Somehow, the staff let some couples in and seated them at someone else's table without asking the person sitting there first, but we stood in line cause we didn't want to sit with anyone else.

I also took one star because there were cockroaches in our room 🪳. Almost every day, we killed 2-3 small roaches in the bathroom. Once, when I got out of the shower, I wanted to reach into my bag, and there was a huge orange cockroach in it. I was scared, threw the bag on the floor, and killed it. It was horrible. Honestly, after that, I felt disgusted to be in that place.

The guys at the reception were quite friendly. I wouldn't say that they were rude to us. Everyone communicated calmly and normally. The water in the pool was quite cool, so I didn't swim there, but my boyfriend did.

And also this is a good location for reach some places. Let’s say in the middle of the city. We took taxi all the time and once even a metro.

So I would say this hotel was 3/5.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-17T10:17:25.244879365Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Romario Allen", "rating": 4, "text": "Overall, they are worth the four stars. Great location, like the art deco style of the establishment. Be careful with the elevators, the doors close quicker than what we're used to. The staff were all professional and courteous during our stay, from reception to waitresses to cleaners. Our room would always be cleaned by the lovely cleaning lady (even when we told her it's fine), our towels would be replaced with fresh ones and toiletries restocked. They have two different dinning style at the hotel: Biergarten and Urban Café. Biergarten is located outside with a view of the pool and stairs leading up to the jacuzzi and sundeck. It has a more casual, open bar feel.  Whereas the latter, feels more upscale with classical, jazz music (also where you're served breakfast in a buffet style). The pool was surprisingly not that cold at night, which was great. Downside, the jacuzzi could have been warmer. The gym was smaller than what I thought it would be in person and some of the machines can use an upgrade.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-01T14:49:11.271505999Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "Nicole A", "rating": 3, "text": "Rooms were dated, dark and small with no refrigerator.  Air conditioner didn't work. The service on the other hand was outstanding! Algis at the front desk the bellmen, and housekeeping provided outstanding service. Rooms were clean but there no washcloths. The hardware on the faucets was from the 60s or 70s.  The location was also fantastic.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-05T18:35:02.086631919Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Kiela Smith", "rating": 5, "text": "This place was the perfect fit for my cousin & I after a horrible experience at a nearby hotel that we left to come here in the middle of the night. Riande is an oasis. I love everything about it. Attention to detail, artsy vibe, tons of plants outdoors makes you feel that you’re not in an urban concrete jungle. The pool was long enough for me to swim laps and adequate lounge chairs + tables & chairs to lay in the sun & take a siesta after swimming. Waitress also came with menu for food & drinks from the restaurant. Exercise gym with equipment, free breakfast, restaurant, bar & beer garden outside with huge outdoor screen, a lovely bathroom accessed from all these areas and multiple decks gave me lots of spaces & activities.
The front desk staff were always a pleasure & helpful. The scent was lovely throughout the hotel. If I could say one thing to know is that the 2 double beds were comfortable but room felt tight yet workable for 2 people. Had small shared bedside table, desk & chair + extended desk + surface for suitcase & shelf under it, small bathroom, closet & cubbies clothes & safe to protect valuables.
Curious to try the suite next time I stay here as this will be my first choice for an affordable, quality hotel oasis. I left to go to Bogota & my cousin stayed 2 more days and changed to a king bed room with pool view that she loved.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-26T11:09:34.421123853Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Atl_Elephant1224", "rating": 4, "text": "​The Riande Urban is in a solid location with a grocery store nearby and plenty of great food options within walking distance. The staff were very welcoming, and the amenities like the pretty pool and the rooftop jacuzzi are nice perks.
​However, the property is showing its age. The furniture is quite outdated, even in the common areas, and I noticed the bonded leather (synthetic) upholstery starting to peel and crack. It was a reasonable stay for the price, but we were looking for something a bit more modern.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-19T15:33:03.243703591Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}]

address: C. San Lucas, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: hamburger_restaurant, wine_bar, coffee_shop, pizza_restaurant, cafe, seafood_restaurant, latin_american_restaurant, pastry_shop, dessert_shop, bakery, american_restaurant, confectionery, bar, restaurant, point_of_interest, food_store, store, food, establishment
reviews_json: [{"author": "Kid JL", "rating": 3, "text": "There's a nice and slow music playing which makes the vibe quite cozy and enjoyable. I do feel like some of the interior decorations do not really match the vibe but it's not unbearable either. The service was decent. The waitress that showed us to our table and that took our order seemed a bit gloomy. They were definitely not on the  most cheerful. The food itself was decent. I especially liked the presentation and the flavours of the pina calada. The rest of the food was a bit too salty for me and the empanadas were not what I was expecting. The exterior was heavily fried and there was very little filling which made it a bit less enjoyable.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-02T01:26:50.922439705Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Toni Jackson", "rating": 5, "text": "I had a really fantastic solo lunch at this location. The staff were so warm and friendly. The food was amazing. The cocktail I had was strong and delicious.. the price of the drink was unbeatable. It was also a great opportunity to work on my Spanish! I will be back!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-27T23:32:06.663435815Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Giovanni Li Manni", "rating": 5, "text": "Very relaxing vibe, no hurry at any time. Waitress welcomed us with a unique chicken soup right away. One of the best soups ever and it was free! The rest of the food was as delicious. Prices reasonable and taste is really authentic (which I must say nowadays is rare in Panama!). And the owner has some Italian connections… so if you are up for a sudden twist and you are up for buffalo mozzarella or an espresso, well you are also covered! But I did not try Italian dishes as I was desperately craving for local cuisine… and I think I got as close as possible 😉", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-28T00:28:14.137411855Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "Keeks", "rating": 4, "text": "Concolon was a decent place near where we were staying. We really enjoyed the appetizer with patacones with cheese dipped in aji chombo (Panamanian salsa) and give that 5/5. The concolon with roja vieja, rice and beans was 4/5. The crispy rice really added a nice texture to the otherwise a soft textured dish. The ropa vieja was tender but on the blander side. I added some hot sauce from the table to make it more flavorful. The plantain lasagna was a nice surprise, however the sweet note of the plantains makes it harder to consider it a proper main dish than a dessert. Nice atmosphere, however pretty empty for a Friday evening.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-12T20:06:32.985997969Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}, {"author": "G W", "rating": 4, "text": "The food here is delicious! name: Cantina del Tigre
address: PLAZA CENTENARIO, C. 68 Este, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "RiCK S", "rating": 3, "text": "Ranked among the top 50 in Latin America, the restaurant initially impressed with its Caribbean fish tempura, which was genuinely delicious. I added bone marrow on the side, but unfortunately both the bone marrow and my partner’s dish were far too salty for my taste.

The service was basic and unremarkable. Given the price point and reputation, I expected a higher level of consistency and overall quality.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-16T00:49:01.281581615Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Todd", "rating": 1, "text": "The food here is genuinely above average and worth trying—the kitchen is efficient, with my order arriving in under 20 minutes. The cocktails were also phenomenal. Unfortunately, the service was some of the worst I’ve experienced.

After my food was dropped, I was never checked on again. I sat for 40 minutes with empty plates and an empty glass while every surrounding table was serviced multiple times; by the time I flagged someone else down, it had been 60 minutes since my server was last at the table. I had intended to order a glass of wine and dessert, but never got the chance. I eventually had to find a different staff member just to get the bill.

Most concerningly: when my server finally appeared for payment, she confirmed the tip amount twice and then intentionally entered a higher gratuity than authorized. Having spent seven years as a bartender, I know the difference between a slammed floor and a lack of effort. Forgetting a guest for an hour in a half-empty, slow restaurant and then tampering with a tip is completely unacceptable.

For management - I dined on 4/1 (8-10pm) and my server was a woman with a pink headband and white nails. Please get her service corrected as it’s materially brining down the business.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-02T03:37:06.747101327Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "Megan Leung", "rating": 5, "text": "Was recommended Cantina del Tigre along with a few others by our foodie Uber driver. We were so glad to follow his advice for our last meal in Panama before flying out. The fish ceviche is incredible, mango pineapple salad is so refreshing, and the fried plantains and charred octopus were showstoppers. Everything was beautifully presented and we will be recommending this to any friend or family coming to Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-28T18:12:14.739198163Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "AJM", "rating": 5, "text": "The besca al carbon was excellent..no notes. Good enough for two people to eat and be quite stuffed. The apps were also unique and tasty. Would def go againm", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-19T20:01:37.750740109Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "T L", "rating": 5, "text": "A lovely experience — we couldn’t fault a thing. I can see why it’s rated #40 in the Top 50 Best Restaurants in Latin America for 2025.

We arrived at 12:30 pm without a reservation and were lucky to get a seat, as the place started filling up slowly after 1pm.

Our waitress recommended the 2.5–3 lb chargrilled catch of the day (seabass) to share between two people — the portion was generous and deliciously satisfying. I also had the bone marrow to start, and it was good!

Overall rating: 5/5", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-24T22:30:19.877779420Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}]

[{"author": "FrosT", "rating": 5, "text": "We're finally seeing the electronic order and payment system in Panama, bravo!

The door also has those feet thingys so you don't have to touch it... name: La Arepería Panamá
address: 07099, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 7:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 11:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Jose Fernandez", "rating": 5, "text": "I recently tried their meat soup, and let me tell you, it was absolutely phenomenal! The flavors were rich and comforting, making each spoonful a delight. I highly recommend giving it a try—you won't be disappointed!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-04-06T18:40:37.493740Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "JB Degustador", "rating": 5, "text": "Amazing
Brisket Arepa
The brisket arepa at La Areperia Panama is a standout dish that combines traditional Venezuelan flavors with a modern twist. The arepa itself is made from cornmeal, grilled to perfection with a slightly crispy exterior and a soft, warm interior. The star of this dish is undoubtedly the brisket filling, which is tender, juicy, and packed with flavor. The meat is slow-cooked to achieve a melt-in-your-mouth texture, and it’s generously portioned inside the arepa.
The brisket is typically seasoned with a blend of spices that complement the natural flavors of the meat without overpowering it. The combination of the hearty brisket and the slightly sweet corn flavor of the arepa creates a delightful balance of tastes and textures.

Cachapa
The cachapa at La Areperia Panama is another must-try item on their menu. This traditional Venezuelan dish is a sweet corn pancake that’s crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. At La Areperia, the cachapa is made fresh to order, ensuring that each bite is warm and full of flavor.
The cachapa is typically filled with queso de mano, a soft, slightly salty Venezuelan cheese that melts beautifully inside the warm pancake. The contrast between the sweet corn and the savory cheese creates a harmonious flavor profile that’s both comforting and satisfying.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-03-20T05:34:52.177710Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Jahir Sanchez", "rating": 5, "text": "the best arepas in Panama, many Venezuelan option as well as street food, all quality, every week it is my obligatory stop to eat, delicious, no doubt.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-09-16T22:40:41.162515Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Albert Osyp", "rating": 5, "text": "At this place you can find all the Venezuela ln food and street food with a good quality of the product. It is simply an amazing experience and incredible services, you need to go there if you visit panama 💯", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-12-27T11:47:33.544771Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Neftalí González Saldaña", "rating": 5, "text": "Such a great place to eat great food", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-03-13T07:18:31.670854Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}]

schedule: Monday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Abigail Howe", "rating": 5, "text": "Loved this place so much I went back the NEXT DAY!! I work remote and loved this coffee shop with free WiFi! I’m a coffee and tea snob…lol. Specialty coffee, espresso, teas, breakfast, lunch, desserts, you name it, they’ve got it! I had a latte and the pistachio chocolate chip cookie the first day. The second day was an iced rose matcha! SoooOooo yummy! The vibe inside is ON POINT! Everyone is super friendly! 6 out of 5 stars! Don’t miss out on this GEM!! 😍", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-07T20:57:46.969002934Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "jose luis villegas londoño", "rating": 5, "text": "Mentiritas Blancas: Panama’s Premier Coffee Destination
Mentiritas Blancas represents the pinnacle of Panama’s specialty coffee culture, seamlessly blending artisanal precision with a sophisticated culinary program. Renowned for its world-class bean selection and expert brewing techniques, the establishment offers an unparalleled sensory experience where every pour-over and gourmet dish is executed to perfection. It is, without question, the definitive destination for those seeking the highest standards of coffee and cuisine in the city.
Beyond the menu, the venue distinguishes itself through a culture of exceptional hospitality and modern professionalism. The staff’s deep expertise and intuitive service create an atmosphere that is as welcoming as it is refined, setting a benchmark for the Panamanian service industry.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-21T14:21:44.042102540Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Rahul Goyal", "rating": 5, "text": "It was my first time visit to Panama city and I can proudly say that as an ardent coffee fan, trying the Geisha Coffee at ‘Mentiritas Blancas’ at Via Argentina Branch was my best till date. Special thanks to Cesar and Jay for creating the best coffee experience for me as they explained me in detail about Geisha Coffee and how to actually have it. This is something extraordinary experience. Accross the Globe I travelled and never ever had such a detailed description to enjoy Coffee. Thank you ‘Mentiritas Blancas’ for making it special", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-29T23:46:27.593887962Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "______", "rating": 5, "text": "Must try for brunch/breakfast! I stopped by for coffee one day with no expectations and was blown away. I had to come back for a second time in my short stay in Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-11T06:20:59.452071101Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Johnny Barros", "rating": 4, "text": "Great food.

name: Los Años Locos | San Francisco

schedule: Monday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Leslie Whitlow", "rating": 1, "text": "Visited for the first time for a glass of wine and an appetizer.  For my appetizer I choose the Fried Calamari.  The wine was excellent. The fried Calamari was exceptionally bad due to being not only ALL tentacles but they were so over fried they were dry and hard.
I realize steak is their thing but if you can't get the $13 Calamari right who wants to gamble on a steak?
Waiter was nice but when I questioned him about the Calamari he said it was normal. Photo attached.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-18T00:34:26.698456845Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "YoSoyBallito", "rating": 5, "text": "Amazing food, great wine, and excellent service—this place has it all. Whether you want to enjoy a relaxed meal or have a fun night out, it doubles as a sports bar and a perfect Argentinian grill. Located in San Francisco, Panama City, it offers everything from juicy grilled beef and flavorful chorizo to delicious pasta dishes. Every bite is full of authentic taste and perfectly cooked. One of the highlights is the included salad bar, which adds even more value to your meal. If you’re craving quality food in a lively atmosphere, this is a spot you’ll definitely enjoy.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-05-17T21:30:45.713763Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Ana Ortiz", "rating": 4, "text": "The food is very tasty but depending on what you orden, the portions can be either huge or small.
The menu has a lot of options! We ordered sea food but saw that meat is the house specialty.
The service is a bit slow, so consider this if you arrive already hungry!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-03-10T21:13:48.305952Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Lenrock", "rating": 5, "text": "We finally made it,and it didn't disappoint.  We had an exceptional lunch at Los Años Locos. The Los Años Filet was cooked to a perfect temperature, and the Cobia was incredibly flavorful.

One of the best parts of their lunch service is the flexibility; we both opted for half-orders, which turned out to be the perfect portion size since a fresh salad bar and bread service are included. The architecture of the restaurant is gorgeous—be sure to take a peek at their impressive wine vault. The service was attentive without being intrusive. I’m already looking forward to my next visit.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-12T18:35:48.165223932Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Roman Kvasov", "rating": 5, "text": "Alright! Lets make It clear. I would give 6/5 stars if the would be possible.

The service was great and the food very tasty. The steaks were done as they were ordered and we received a lot of food. The portions are huge and the price as fine.

As we walked out we bought a bottle of wine and were asked if we have an opener at home. We couldn't answer so we just got an opener as a gift. Amazing experience!

We definitely come again and this is now on our favourite places list.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-01-20T17:22:32.785175Z", "relative_time": "3 years ago"}]

schedule: Monday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Freedom Thompson", "rating": 5, "text": "I am a regular customer; 3 to 4 times a week had an issue with receiving my meal and coffee at the same time. I wrote an extended review which was noticed.
28 October 2025 *Update*
One of the managers at Athanasiou reached out to me about my review, we discussed, in detail, my concerns and she asked if I would allow them to correct any issues. I agreed and everything has been perfect each visit. I appreciate the effort in ensuring that all customers receive good service and they make the corrections to continue a mutually beneficial relationship. Thank you Yessica for going to extra mile and thank you again, Jocelyn for keeping my order correct.
And thank you to the remaining staff, I don't know everyone's name yet, however, I recognize that it is a team effort. I appreciate it.
***2nd update. 16 November 2025. Everything is perfect with service, food, atmosphere. I am very thankful to both managers and all staff.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-17T02:05:25.028792762Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Simone Calandra", "rating": 4, "text": "Athanasiou is really good, regardless of which branch you pick. I recommend this place to everyone.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-10-17T19:54:34.307204Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}, {"author": "Lisa Brohman", "rating": 4, "text": "Food and atmosphere are great but customer service needs improvement. I ordered a drink that I found too bitter and they made no effort to fix it. They took it and told me to buy a new one, I asked for it back and to add a shot of syrup, they told me I had to get back in line to pay for it.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-21T18:44:48.818333315Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Iya Ogbedi", "rating": 2, "text": "I’m have been coming to this cafe for the last four days. I’m standing in front of the restaurant leaving this review.

I literally spent $30-$40 every time I come into this place this morning.

I just asked your supervisor if I could have salad no bread no salmon and an egg meaning take away the bread take away the salmon give me more salad because I’ve been eating too much bread and I’m disabled.

She said no we’re gonna charge you extra. it makes no sense.

I do NOT understand where you’re coming from I didn’t want all the extra things.

I wanted the salad an egg and that’s it so now I’m never coming in this place again. I am gonna tell everybody I know in both countries to not support.

It’s actually asinine. I spent $120 in this restaurant in the last five days and all I asked was for a small adjustment.

I didn’t want all that bread because the bread is not the best and I didn’t want the salmon and she wanted to charge me 2 to 3 dollars extra for a handful of lettuce.

My suggestion add a salad option to your petite breakfast menu.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-12T14:27:09.207691015Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}]
