name: La Pulpería - San Francisco
address: al lado de Tomillo, C. 71 Este, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Don R Campbell", "rating": 5, "text": "A great cocktail bar, with perfect lighting and decent food.

When in Panama City one is told to find hidden gems in the San Francisco neighborhood. This is definitely one of the top choices.

Friendly, welcoming staff with creativity.

Cocktails were so well crafted that we stayed for food (despite having other plans).

We tried a few of their classic cocktails and all were a win. Especially the Hanky Panky and the French 76.

The food was 'decent' but quite cold. We had ribs and meat skewers.

Every one of the staff were friendly and engaging.

Definitely a good find", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-27T03:45:48.606519514Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Cici", "rating": 5, "text": "The service, food and vibe of this place is spectacular. We came in for the Happy Hour menu which had an array of various drinks for a great price.  We then ordered the chicken lollipops in the sweet chili flavor, the mac and cheese with cauliflower, and the empanadas. The lollipops were a good portion and we enjoyed those the most. 10/10 The mac and cheese was a good flavor but had more sauce than pasta so I would give it a 8/10 because I liked the fried cauliflower, and the empanadas were a large portion but I didn’t particularly care for the filling, so I would give it a 6/10. The drinks however, were a 10/10! For happy hour, the drinks were very well priced, regular sized, strong, and not watered down. We will definitely return to try more menu offerings as well as happy hour drinks😊🥃", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-06T17:05:40.554517213Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "J Nordy", "rating": 5, "text": "La Pulpería – outstanding in every respect: staff, ambience and music, food, drinks, and overall style!

I recently saw a review in which a party of four left the restaurant still hungry; that was not our experience at all.

We ordered four drinks from the happy‑hour menu plus two regular‑priced drinks. We also shared the falafel with dips, sweet‑chili lollipops, hummus topped with steak, and the carpaccio—all of which were superb! (Our bill was $81 plus tip)

Drinks:
Happy Hour Menu (before 7p) the Old Fashioned was traditional with a subtle hint of vanilla, the Margarita was light and sweet (not my favorite but good) and the French 76 was super delicious. The El Sobrino was phenomenal and perfect to finish the meal as my dessert.

We will definitely return to La Pulpería the next time we’re in PC!!

*we did make a reservation and I’m glad we did because the place filled up after 7", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-06T20:16:34.852312321Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Dianna escool", "rating": 5, "text": "Incredible food and experience. We were looking for a place to go at night (very late like 11) and La Pulperia was amazing. They treat us very well. Juan Luis was very helpful with the recommendations.
The terrace has a nice weather.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-03T04:42:16.433671403Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Karla Capote", "rating": 5, "text": "My experience here was 10/10 the staff here is amazing and the service is great. If you are ever in Panama I recommended this spot you will have an amazing time. Huge shoutout to our server Charlie for making the experience even better.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-26T00:47:25.182927996Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}]

schedule: Monday: 7:30 PM – 2:30 AM | Tuesday: 7:30 PM – 2:30 AM | Wednesday: 7:30 PM – 2:30 AM | Thursday: 6:00 PM – 4:00 AM | Friday: 6:00 PM – 4:00 AM | Saturday: 7:00 PM – 4:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Génesis Pineda", "rating": 5, "text": "We had such a great time at Green Room Karaoke! The food was surprisingly amazing, we ordered tostones, chicharrones, and empanadas, and everything was delicious and fresh.

If you want to sing and maybe embarrass yourself (in the most fun way) with your friends without too many people watching, I definitely recommend going early. We went on a Tuesday, and it started filling up around 10:30 PM. The vibe is amazing, the audience is super friendly and supportive, which makes it even more fun to get up there and sing your heart out. We sang a lot and had the best time. Highly recommend for a fun night out!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-04T04:16:52.238346599Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Orie", "rating": 5, "text": "This place is a VIBE! The owner Luis is so sweet. The food was delicious (I was to hungry to take a picture!!! I'm not sure I even chewed! )and the service was very quick. Whoever made those ribs that slid off the bone must have angelic hands! They host private events, live music and is open to whoever wants to jam out. The parking fills fast, but the Uber will get you there and back to your room with no problem. The taxi is ready to give you a ride as well. I'll be back 🥰😍", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-12-07T22:37:02.801847Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Jay De La", "rating": 4, "text": "I traveled from another country to celebrate my friend’s birthday at Green Room Karaoke in Panama City. Overall, the venue and atmosphere were great, and we were really looking forward to enjoying karaoke.

Unfortunately, the karaoke DJ’s behavior was disappointing. He clearly prioritized his friends, skipped other customers in the rotation, and allowed the same people to sing repeatedly.

This took away from the experience for those of us who came specifically to enjoy karaoke.
Karaoke should be fair and fun for everyone, especially for new customers visiting for the first time.

I would recommend that management speak with the DJ about treating all guests with the same respect and courtesy, without skipping the line or favoring friends. With more fairness and professionalism, this place could be an excellent experience for everyone.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-04T06:48:02.364750715Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Mayhell Coronado", "rating": 5, "text": "Excelente ambiente. Small, cozy and super fun!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2017-06-11T16:20:02.285Z", "relative_time": "9 years ago"}, {"author": "Jacks Clarks", "rating": 5, "text": "geat vibe decent prices welcoming staff. reviews_json:
[{"author": "Paty Hohmann", "rating": 5, "text": "Beautiful dark lit ambient and decoration, super tasty square shaped pizza and great wine selection, even if the waiter first tried to push a $120 bottle on to us.
Everything was great, I would definitely go back again.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-04-17T19:12:13.909927Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Cynthia B", "rating": 4, "text": "We recently visited this restaurant and had an overall enjoyable experience, though there were a few areas for improvement.

Parking & Atmosphere:
The convenience of valet and free parking was a great start to the visit. There’s no dress code, making it a relaxed and welcoming spot. However, the music was slightly too loud, which made conversation a bit difficult at times. Additionally, it’s quite cold inside, so I’d recommend bringing a jacket if you plan to sit indoors.

Service:
The service had its ups and downs. On the positive side, they were attentive to individual preferences, such as recommending vegan options and thoughtfully asking when to serve the soup. However, when a mix-up occurred with one guest's order, the server insisted it was the guest's mistake rather than addressing it more amicably.

Food:
The menu offered a mix of hits and misses:

Carpaccio: Nicely seasoned and flavorful, but it lacked enough arugula, which was a bit disappointing.
Soup: The soup was well-seasoned and so enjoyable that it was fully finished. Their thoughtfulness in asking when to serve it was a nice touch.
Pizza: We tried two pizzas – the flatbread pizza and the vegan pizza. The flatbread pizza was flavorful and cooked well, while the vegan pizza had a delicious sauce and fresh toppings. However, the crust was slightly bitter and undercooked at the bottom, likely due to improper oven temperature.
Pasta: The vegan pasta had a nice combination of toppings but was noticeably undersalted. Once we added salt, the dish reached its potential.
Fish: The fish of the day, which was not the dish ordered, was unfortunately too salty. Additionally, the sauce was overwhelming, making it hard to enjoy the meal. The dish was ultimately left unfinished.
Coffee: Two people ordered coffee, but there was inconsistency in flavor – one cup was balanced, while the other was too bitter. Upon requesting a replacement, the issue persisted.
Overall Impression:
Despite some misses with the food and service, we enjoyed the effort to cater to different dietary needs and the overall ambiance. Improvements in the consistency of food quality and a more receptive approach to service mistakes would elevate the experience further.

We’d still consider returning, as the experience had enough positives to make it worthwhile.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-12-27T20:55:37.921347Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "PAT BVAXTER", "rating": 5, "text": "I truly enjoyed my experience at this restaurant.  Pizza is a science at this restaurant and they have one of the best vegan pizzas I've ever had.  The wines are amazing along with the attentive and welcoming staff.  I went twice while staying in Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-30T13:56:59.729347287Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "Connie Ines Chan Guerra", "rating": 5, "text": "We got lunch around 3pm, the restaurant was quite empty ( no reservation needed for this time). This is a good spot for anyone desiring western style comfort food. The olive pizza tasted good and costed only three dollars and fifty cents for a personal pie. name: Niko's Café | El Dorado
address: Av. 17B Nte., Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Panama George", "rating": 4, "text": "Spacious cafeteria restaurant serving panamanian creole food, sandwiches, desserts, pastries, breads, and mediterranean with middle eastern options. This is one of the top franchises with a professional environment. It is a good option for anyone wanting a casual eatery with great sitting area without the fuzz of a full service restaurant.

A walk through the line will expose you to falafels, gyros, creole food such as rice with chicken, fish fillet, roast chicken, sancocho soup, and a bunch of other things. There is also a small bakery section.

Had their creole food late at midnight and it was decent. The four items that I had - fried chicken, yellow rice, lentils and salad - tasted okay. Then on my second visit I tried the club sandwich. There must have been something wrong because the meat tasted bad. The omelette, though, was very good.

It had been exactly thirty years since I ate at this place. Along that time, more branches were added and changes made along the way. Reading through the reviews, I have to agree with those who lament the present day food. Their original club sandwich set the standard for how delicious such a thing should be. It was amazing back then but it is not the same anymore. Regardless, the food is decent so it should appease most people. Customers are attracted to the pleasant brightly lit environment, service and cleanliness. This is the only 24-hour place in the vicinity of its type.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-08-17T05:26:02.537782Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}, {"author": "Gabriel Thomas", "rating": 5, "text": "Nikos cafe Panama is one of the best places to eat. address: Centro Comercial Los Tucanes, Av. Miguel A. Brostella, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: chinese_restaurant, breakfast_restaurant, dim_sum_restaurant, banquet_hall, wedding_venue, cantonese_restaurant, bakery, meal_delivery, food_delivery, meal_takeaway, food_store, event_venue, store, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, service, establishment
schedule: Monday: 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Keeks", "rating": 3, "text": "Classic dimsum served in a banquet hall style Chinese restaurant in el Dorado, Panama City. While it was very interesting to try new fusion in the dimsum, such as passionfruit coconut pudding or red shrimp and char siu rolls, the flavor wasn’t entirely authentic, which serves the purpose of catering to everybody in Panama. The chicken feet were cooked in a saltier soy sauce than how it’s cooked usually for dimsum. Certainly if you’re looking for authentic Cantonese dimsum, your best bet is Hong Kong Bakery. But I’d say give this at least a try so that you can make your own opinion.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-12T21:06:14.149862189Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}, {"author": "Lifeooferic", "rating": 5, "text": "Sunly Restaurant is a long-standing favorite, particularly renowned for its authentic Cantonese cuisine and traditional Dim Sum experience, which is often served tableside via push carts—a delightful and rare tradition.
​The food is consistently praised for being delicious, fresh, and served in generous portions (many dishes are large enough to share). Favorites include the shrimp wonton, sweet and sour chicken, and the wide selection of Dim Sum.
​The restaurant boasts a large, spacious environment, making it perfect for both large family gatherings and special events, with private VIP rooms and event spaces available.
​The main takeaway is fantastic food and atmosphere, but be prepared for potentially chaotic service during peak hours (especially weekend Dim Sum), as the staff can sometimes seem overwhelmed. If you're looking for flavorful, authentic Chinese food in a classic setting, Sunly is a must-try.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-06T14:57:43.527186984Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "Maria Luisa Rakai", "rating": 5, "text": "Never disappoints.  The food here is amazing.  Service is outstanding.  And the price is very affordable.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-06-25T11:17:29.643088963Z", "relative_time": "11 months ago"}, {"author": "Panama George", "rating": 5, "text": "This is one of the best places for dim sum if you like spacious and fancy. A remarkable restaurant serving traditional Chinese food in a spacious environment of two floors. Large dining hall and private rooms on the main floor. Event space and sunday dim sum above. This is one of a few places that you will see dim sum being strolled around on push carts, much to the delight of aficionados. It resides in the middle of a busy commercial area and just around the corner to the very first indoor mall.

Parking is not for free. The machine at the entrance will dispense a white card when you drive in. Give it to the restaurant staff and they will scan it, effectively canceling the parking fee.

A bit of trivia. Their original restaurant under a different name started in the original chinatown. Amazingly, it still stands there to this day. In fact, it is the only original restaurant in avenue B that is still in business after all these decades. A testament to the family's culinary prowess.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-08-22T04:04:42.183027Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}, {"author": "Danny Danny", "rating": 5, "text": "This place definitely lives up to its reputation in being one of the best Chinese restaurants in Panama!  Food was so fresh and tasty and the service was outstanding! I can't wait to go back!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2018-05-24T20:20:39.138Z", "relative_time": "8 years ago"}]

This Bakery is a self service place which allows you to walk around and choose the types of bread you want and they provide you thongs and a tray. There were so many bread option to choose from such as sweets, savory and much more. One of my favorites would be the one with sausage and another one with the egg spread on top of the bread. There we also drinks to buy such as soda,juice and even boba! The atmosphere was welcoming with beautiful pink flowers as decoration. Overall, a great place to get different type of bread but one recommendation I suggest is having 3 people in the counter. Two people is register and one person attending people who want boba since when rush hour comes there were a long line and waiting atleast 10 minutes to check out.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-04-18T16:19:32.965987Z", "relative_time": "3 years ago"}, {"author": "Panama George", "rating": 5, "text": "Premium Asian bakery serving drinks and pastries in a beautiful air conditioned setting. Large relaxed seating area with lots of natural light.

Here is one of the most comfortable bakeries in the city. Recently renovated and very clean, this is a good place to sit and relax. I like their jasmine with lychee fruit tea. Also the Vietnamese style iced coffee. This is one of only three places that I know that sell dalgona coffee.

The pastries they sell are mostly classic Asian options. The most common one that I have seen around the world being the polo bun with coconut. The cakes and desserts range from tiramisu, to passion fruit mousse, to green tea and many others. They also have homemade cookies and crackers which I always buy. Bubble tees, fruit drinks and coffee drinks.

Electrical outlets throughout the seating area. The only issue is the lack of parking spaces but good thing that there are independent parking attendants to assist direct the flow of traffic. Give them a tip.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-07-28T17:37:06.066184Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}]

name: Don Lee | El Dorado
address: Vía Ricardo J. Alfaro, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Panama George", "rating": 5, "text": "Delicious food served fast hassle-free in a beautiful interior fit for a top franchise.

This is one of those places that are on top of my list every time I visit the country. Not only because it is delicious, but rather because the taste is unique. I cannot possibly explain it. I crave this panamanianized chinese food. My recommendation is the number 4 leon pan mein which is tender noodles with meat and pan fried egg added on top. Add an order of the arroz con piña drink. Finish it all with the arroz con leche dessert. The entire menu is delicious.

Fast, well-trained service. Great food. Air-conditioned interior with comfortable seats. Ornaments and statues for added effect.

It has to be stated that the owners are very courteous to their customers. Simply look at the table arrangement and see the variety of seating options. They even have a large table that fits a party of eight. Even the bathrooms are well kept. Service is top class.

It also has to be stated that these folks are very forward-thinking. First and foremost, they were the first to start serving this type of panamanianized chinese food. They also have rewards points, a cell phone app and parking lot with spaces dedicated to online orders. I was served my meal by the country's first ever robot waiter. It acted very professional and friendly.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-10-26T00:11:21.459130Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}, {"author": "Hilda Snowden", "rating": 5, "text": "Good food. Also, definitely some of the best coffee I’ve had while in Panama!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-06T19:04:11.960932375Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "xibjou", "rating": 5, "text": "I had the Salmon Benedict. Yikes it was good. Also a Macchiato because I knew beforehand it was traditional. It was very good. Many reviewers say service is slow but that was definitely not my experience. It was faster than it needed to be but the dish was still excellent! I am from California so price is different for me than most of the world. It was 30% cheaper than outside San Francisco and 40%  cheaper than inside SF. This is a metropolitan city so I say good price. That is all", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-04T05:20:51.304566437Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Ariel Felipe Torres", "rating": 4, "text": "Well, I think it’s a place with a pretty nice and simple design; it gives you a sense of peace. However, the food didn’t fully convince me. I went for the Burger Week special, and it was good, but that was about it.

The wings that the person I went with ordered were much better—great sauce—but the extra fries we ordered arrived long after the wings.

The girl who served us got my drink wrong, but she provided really good service and always had a great attitude, despite the place being packed and even having a line outside. That really surprised me.

I would come back for a coffee or to try other options.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-04-01T20:21:40.670420Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "NICK MENNELL", "rating": 3, "text": "The breakfast that empties your wallet but fills your belly.

Look, I’ll give it to Kokomo - they make one heck of a breakfast (the Shakshuka and salmon Benedict in particular). Seriously, if you’re looking for the best morning meal in Panama City, this place delivers on flavor. The food is genuinely great (for Panama), and the atmosphere has that perfect “I’m on vacation even though I live here” vibe that makes you want to linger over your coffee.

But it goes sideways when it comes to price and service. Kokomo operates on what I like to call the “airline pricing model” - everything costs extra, and I mean everything. Want butter for your toast? That’ll be $2. Almond milk instead of dairy? Another $1. I half expected them to charge me for looking at the menu too long.

The service, bless their hearts, moves at the speed of molasses in winter (also typical for Panama). I’ve seen glaciers advance faster than our server attended our table. It’s like they’re all operating on island time, which would be charming if I had island time and we were on an island.

Don’t get me wrong - when the food finally arrives, it’s worth the wait (and the small business loan you’ll need to take out). The vibe really is stellar, with that relaxed feel that makes you forget you’re slowly going bankrupt over eggs Benedict.

Would I go back? Probably, but I’d bring a second mortgage application just in case I want extra ice.

Bottom line: Great food, great atmosphere, not-so-great for your bank account or your patience. Come hungry, come wealthy, and come with low expectations for speedy and attentive service.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-05-23T15:43:53.882886Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Camila Gomez", "rating": 4, "text": "Went in for brunch, restaurant is usually pretty packed and gets a bit hot inside. Food was good we had the friend chicken and waffles, and tio Japa. Coffee was good!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-12-24T15:41:11.863386Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}]

H.", "rating": 2, "text": "This eatery offers an array of Barbecue beefs and seafood, however, the food preparation and presentation is subpar. I believe the price/quality relation is unfair. Service could also be improved, our waitress was new and she did not have any kind of menu knowledge or training, which wasn’t a good first impression. I believe there are nicer BBQ experiences in Panamá 🇵🇦", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-08-25T22:22:15.740934Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Joey Fallas", "rating": 2, "text": "What a disappointment.

I understand this is meant to be a BBQ restaurant, not fine dining. However, I became skeptical as soon as I saw how extensive the menu was. Most good BBQ places typically offer a limited selection and focus on executing it well. BBQ Fellas has far too many protein options, which raised concerns from the start.

I went today with a colleague to try it out. Surprisingly, there was no smell of smoke upon arrival. Inside, the dining room was extremely warm. The menu is only available via QR code.

We were both hungry, so we ordered from both the lunch special menu and the regular menu. I ordered the quarter chicken from the lunch menu and, assuming this was a serious BBQ establishment, added a half-pound of brisket as a benchmark for any respectable BBQ spot.

The chicken was not bad and came with a side of patacones. However, the patacones were clearly fried much earlier—possibly even days before. They were dry and very chewy.

The brisket looked promising when it arrived, but there was no smoke flavor, no bark, and it was dry. It gave the impression that it had been cooked days earlier in an oven rather than a smoker, then reheated.
The buns were rock hard—either stale from sitting out for days or previously frozen and microwaved.

If this is considered one of the best BBQ spots in Panama, then the standards are extremely low.

We will not be returning.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-01T19:40:46.722979844Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Carolyne Palomo", "rating": 5, "text": "The food was absolutely delicious. We started with a brisket, which melted in our mouth and was perfectly seasoned. For the main course, we ordered ribs and wings — both flawless as well. We finished with a chocolate cake that was the perfect ending to the meal.

On top of that, our server Brian was incredibly friendly and guided us from start to finish with spot-on recommendations of wonderfully flavorful dishes.

Highly recommended. It was our first time here, but we’ll definitely be coming back", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-07T00:32:57.539887425Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Sherri Parker", "rating": 5, "text": "Awesome bbq. The service was great as well. Portion sizes are large and worth the price. We will definitely be returning.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-09-06T02:19:43.793416Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}]

[{"author": "Je Se", "rating": 4, "text": "Good empanadas. They consider them a patty. The variety of choice is small, though. For pattys they had: beef and chicken. For tarts: pineapple and plantain. That's about it. They must have been losing money trying to sell other food items (see reviewers' photos) and cut selection down to a few best sellers.

Pictured, the tarts are the ones with the piercings in the top section of the tart (clockwise), starting at 12:00: cod cake, chicken patty, pineapple tart, plantain tart. The beef patty looks identical to the chicken patty.

There is little seating inside, unfortunately. They must have downsized from what I'm guessing based on the older photos. Parking is extremely limited too.

*Word of warning: watch these guys, the menu they have on the display screen will show the actual price. However, they'll try to charge foreigners the GRINGO FARE and mark your order up 65%!

name: Popino Restaurante
address: PH Wanders & Yoo, C. 56 Este, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 5:00 – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Sylvain Partisotti", "rating": 5, "text": "Popino is hands down one of the best restaurants in Panama City – if not the best.

We came here for a romantic dinner date, and it was the perfect choice.

The food was outstanding from start to finish. Everything was impeccably fresh, and well presented and the taste was wonderful.

Paired with a great red wine recommended by the sommelier, who nailed our preferences perfectly.

Service was top-notch – our server Angel was attentive, friendly, and kept everything flowing smoothly, from refilling glasses to timing the courses just right.

The atmosphere is beautiful: elegant yet cozy, with music at the perfect volume for conversation. It’s intimate and sophisticated without feeling stuffy.
It is absolutely worth every penny for the quality and overall experience.

Highly recommend Popino for couples looking for a special night out, or even adult groups. We’ll be back!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-29T02:03:14.010195028Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Aleksandra P", "rating": 5, "text": "Omg!!! This place is ridiculously amazing! Absolutely to die for! I’ve tried all the top restaurants in Panama and this was hands down the best one and I’m pretty hard to please. We had octopus, popino seabass and tuna steak and it’s really hard to pick the favorite. The passion fruit dessert is a must as well! The ambience is beautiful and service very knowledgeable and friendly.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-26T02:15:41.049576589Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Matthew Bagnall", "rating": 5, "text": "Fabulous and classy experience here with outstanding service and delicious food. All the servers treated us so well, and the sommelier recommended a beautiful bottle of wine for us. The entraña was seasoned and cooked perfectly, and the fettuccine ragú was so flavorful and delicious. One of the best restaurants and experiences in Panama City.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-04T00:00:29.995777814Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Nahdin Sabla", "rating": 5, "text": "I celebrated my birthday at Popino and had a truly lovely experience. I made a reservation in advance, and the service was excellent from start to finish. The ambiance is elegant and stylish — a perfect spot for a special night out.

We shared several dishes and everything was delicious. My favorites were the grilled octopus and the grilled shrimp — both perfectly cooked and full of flavor. We also had the cheese cappelletti and the gnocchi with brisket, which were rich and comforting. For dessert, the lemon meringue was fresh and satisfying.

Around 8 p.m. a DJ started playing, which added to the atmosphere. Honestly, the place has so much potential to evolve into a full lounge or after-dinner spot. With a bit more intention behind the music — maybe something more upbeat or electronic later in the evening — it could easily become one of the go-to places in the area for people to stay and enjoy the night after dinner.

One small note: in most places I've celebrated my birthday, they offer a complimentary dessert, which didn’t happen here. Not a big deal, but it would’ve been a sweet touch.

Overall, I had a great time and would definitely recommend Popino for a chic night out in Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-09-17T20:22:46.443967888Z", "relative_time": "9 months ago"}, {"author": "Lisa Shaw", "rating": 5, "text": "Let me start by saying that the service was nothing short of beautiful. My friends and I were on vacation in Panama, staying at Wanders and Yoo, and decided to check out Popino one evening, which turned out to be one of the best decisions of our trip. The first night was so incredible that we found ourselves coming back a second time before we left, just to relive the experience.

From the moment we stepped through the door, we were warmly greeted and instantly felt cared for. Every team member was attentive, gracious, and genuinely invested in making our evening special. But the true standout was Santiago, who went above and beyond in every possible way. Despite our limited Spanish, he anticipated our needs with ease, communicating through kindness, intuition, and professionalism that transcended language barriers. His recommendations were perfect, his timing impeccable, and his genuine warmth made the evening feel personal. You can tell he takes great pride in what he does, offering the kind of service that turns a dinner into a lasting memory.

The food was nothing short of Michelin-star quality. Each dish was beautifully presented and bursting with flavor. From the first bite to the last, you could taste the creativity and care in every detail. The ingredients were fresh, the textures perfectly balanced, and the plating was stunning. It’s rare to find a restaurant that combines innovation with such inviting familiarity, but Popino does it effortlessly.

And just when we thought the evening couldn’t get any better, the restaurant transformed into a vibrant lounge and club as the night went on. The lights softened, the music came alive, and the energy shifted into a chic, electric atmosphere. It was the perfect continuation of an already unforgettable dinner.

We left completely impressed, full, and happy, already planning to return on our next trip to Panama. Whether you’re a traveler looking for a culinary gem or a local searching for a refined yet lively experience, Popino is an absolute must. And if you’re lucky enough to have Santiago as your server, you’ll understand why this place leaves such a lasting impression.

Popino isn’t just a restaurant. It’s an experience.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-07T02:36:31.764408188Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}]

reviews_json:
[{"author": "Cici", "rating": 5, "text": "We enjoyed the service, food, and drinks and this establishment. We received chips with 3 different sauces from mild to hot. We ordered the shrimp and fish tacos. Although We preferred the shrimp tacos flavor and seasoning over the fish, both were good. I ordered the Bacon Truffle burger with truffle fries. The fries were hot, delicious and perfectly crispy. I expected the burger to have more flavor as far as the sauce and accompaniments. There were no lettuce or tomato. I asked for ketchup but maybe it would have benefitted better from bbq sauce and grilled onions or mushrooms. The bacon was nice and crispy though. The drinks were great. I ordered a passionfruit frozen margarita and my partner had a guava drink. Overall we would revisit this spot the next time we are in Panama.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-06T16:56:48.098670406Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Don R Campbell", "rating": 4, "text": "Lunch time escape.

Caught in another rainstorm at lunch time , we jumped into this restaurant. What a nice surprise.

The greeting was welcoming, the Margaritas we're not too sweet, the food plenty and our server was attentive. I'd call that a win.

We ordered off the lunch section of the menu and both of our selections included a "bonus" large salad to start.

I had the Wahaka Tuna Bowl (GF) which was a perfect lunch size.

"She" had the Milenesa Chicken which was a 'hearty' size. Flavor definitely got her approval.

Both were $15 ea.

The decor was 'funky,' the background music was low enough to hold detailed conversation.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-23T21:22:47.865251665Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Dianna escool", "rating": 5, "text": "The food was amazing and the service too!

name: Super Kosher
address: C. Ramon H. Jurado, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: cafe, grocery_store, food_store, store, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment
schedule: Monday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 7:30 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM
reviews_json: [{"author": "Peter Kapshuk", "rating": 5, "text": "This is a wonderful supermarket located on the second level of the Pacific Center complex. I used the elevators on the main level to access the supermarket. Super Kosher is a large, elegant, well managed and well stocked supermarket that has a huge selection of groceries, pharmaceuticals, hygienic and cleaning products. The market includes a pharmacy, wine store, bakery and cafe. It is nicely organized with an impressive variety of items. The produce department has a great selection of fresh produce. As a senior who is health conscious it was nice to see a section dedicated to healthy food products. The staff at the market were all courteous and professional. There is ample parking available in the Pacific Center parking garage. I shopped at Super Kosher on a Sunday afternoon and it was not very busy with immediate availability at the checkout counter. All in an all as a tourist in Panama City, it was a pleasure to shop in such a well maintained, nicely organized and variety rich market.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-18T23:05:43.720992905Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "David Lipowicz", "rating": 5, "text": "Great grocery store, large selection of global kosher items, and they have a dairy and meat restaurant inside. The dairy place is more of a cafe and the meat one is a sit down restaurant. Lots of great options at very reasonable prices! Attached photos of the meat menu.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-30T05:10:22.640230546Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Beth", "rating": 5, "text": "Wanted to give a shoutout to the incredible staff at the dairy restaurant inside of this market. We explained that we were ordering two wrap sandwiches for airplane travel. Despite a language barrier, they took the time to care. They carefully packaged our wraps in an extra layer of plastic. We were so grateful! It’s little acts of kindness that make a place great! The veggie wraps were delicious!!!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-31T04:06:42.953583054Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Kate Holloway", "rating": 4, "text": "A fantastic Kosher Market conveniently located on the second level of the Pacific Center complex, and accessible from the Hospital and Marriott Hotel.  It is a large location with a wide selection of items.  We generally stock up on essentials here, and are in & out of the market quickly and with friendly service.  Online says the market carries halva, but I have been unsuccessful finding it.  That said, we all know how YUMMY this is ... so, it probably sells out quickly!!  :(", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-22T21:04:08.788622095Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Addison Schonland", "rating": 5, "text": "Meat place upstairs and dairy in the store. name: Texas de Brazil
address: C. Tomas Gabriel Duque, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Panama George", "rating": 5, "text": "Excellent representation of how a churrascaria should be managed. Along with a well-presented salad bar, the rodizio service was good. The frequency of service and meats brought to our table was satisfactory.

We had a table of twenty people. Unlike other competing rodizios, this one had a main waiter tending to us most of the time. For that detail alone, I will recommend this place. My only gripe is that the chicken hearts lacked seasoning and cinnamon pineapple was overly sweet.

This is the smallest rodizio I have been to. Hence, the salad bar is small. But its selection of food was sizeable enough. I was happy to see different cheeses, preserved deli slices, feijoada, and decent sushi. Dessert is sold separately, by the way.

Turns out there is valet parking, which is free. You will have to speak to the folks right outside the entrance once you arrive. There is additional parking underground but you will have to pay a bit for that.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-07T05:54:10.308964736Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Tatiana Zelaya", "rating": 4, "text": "Good place to celebrate, food has a good rotation,so it's always fresh. The service of David is very attentive to the customer needs,all the time. They also offer great variety of wines and cocktails.
The 4 Star rating is because we were celebrating Mothers day and had requested upon reserving a bottle of Champagne and Flowers to be set, and that was not there! Musician was delightful!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-12T16:35:28.077055459Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Nihal Etula", "rating": 5, "text": "Nice Brazilian style. Very quality food keeps on coming. No need to mention meat, sausages, ai fell in love with cinnamon banana.

Open buffet is very good as well.

Service was perfect nonstop. Thank you.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-14T23:25:48.467711501Z", "relative_time": "2 months ago"}, {"author": "M. H.", "rating": 2, "text": "Even though this Churrascaría is an all you can eat experience, it should be added “if the taste allows you to”. The salad presentation was subpar with most of the dishes advertised not being offered. The beef cuts were stiff, full of fat and were not tasty. In general, a disappointing gastronomic adventure and the price was extremely high for the quality of the food and service. My recommendation is to skip this restaurant if you can.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-14T23:03:41.978855355Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Mike Grimes", "rating": 5, "text": "Daniel was the server whom was attentive and professional.  Also the head chef Yanelis was also very kind as well as the host & hostess. I was super impressed with the young lady whom charge the bill Ms. Yari noticed an error in our bill that I did not catch and made the correction. This is one of our favorites places to dine.  We appreciate the entire staff.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-31T03:07:16.174513171Z", "relative_time": "2 weeks ago"}]

types: asian_fusion_restaurant, asian_restaurant, fusion_restaurant, sushi_restaurant, bar_and_grill, bar, japanese_restaurant, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment
schedule: Monday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Naz N", "rating": 5, "text": "Brutto was a very special restaurant with flavors unlike any others I’ve seen. Just from the butter for the bread you can tell this restaurant is on a different level. The sushi was amazing, as were the tacos and flatbread. To top it off, the Nutella chocolate ice cream dessert was fantastic. Amazing vibes and lighting throughout the restaurant too. Service was fast and courteous. Highly, highly recommend this spot.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-26T22:24:01.423218183Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "Jim Hazzan", "rating": 5, "text": "The atmosphere was chic and hip! We enjoyed a variety of appetizers which were all very reasonably priced. The food and service was outstanding! We had octopus 🐙 pulpa on a filo dough type crispy shell, cauliflower fried in a barbecue sauce, sweet corn balls, tuna naye or raw which is a medditerran type specialty and pork bau buns! All yummy! Highly recommended!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-04-18T15:05:52.566792476Z", "relative_time": "a month ago"}, {"author": "Aury Fu", "rating": 2, "text": "The Good: Atmosphere & Aesthetics
If you're looking for a beautiful spot to hang out and take some great pictures, this is a fantastic location. The restaurant boasts a wonderful ambiance, a great atmosphere, and beautiful interior design.

The Bad: Food, Service, & Value
Unfortunately, the rest of the experience fell short. A major turn-off was that their full menu wasn't available; in fact, everything I originally wanted to order was out of stock. I settled for the noodles, but they were far too greasy and actually gave me indigestion. The drinks were also pretty underwhelming. Additionally, the service felt a bit lazy and wasn't very helpful.

The Verdict
This place used to be way better, but nowadays, the quality simply doesn't justify the high prices. It's a great spot for the vibe and architecture, but not for the dining experience if that's what you're looking for.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-02T00:27:49.217917608Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Diana Morataya", "rating": 5, "text": "The whole gastronomical experience was wonderful. The atmosphere is perfect for a friends reunion, and the tokens of appreciation from the owner made it a great night. The fusion of flavours like the fish Dish of the day in a corn based souce was brave.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-07-27T04:26:53.059380777Z", "relative_time": "10 months ago"}, {"author": "F M.", "rating": 3, "text": "Brutto stands out for its stylish décor, vibrant ambience, and a diverse menu of food and drinks. The wine list is solid and offers good variety, though unfortunately, the bottles are not stored at the proper temperature, which takes away from the experience. While the food was enjoyable and the setting is great for a night out, the service fell far short. Basic training is clearly lacking; our server, for instance, was unfamiliar with how to take a steak order and had to ask the kitchen if medium rare was even an option. Throughout the evening, the service felt disorganized and intrusive, which was disappointing given the restaurant’s otherwise polished vibe. Brutto has all the ingredients to be excellent, but the team needs training to match the level the restaurant is aiming for.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-06-19T20:13:23.158021820Z", "relative_time": "12 months ago"}]

reviews_json:
[{"author": "Dwight McKabney", "rating": 4, "text": "First about the coffee: Kotowa’s coffee quality is excellent for the price point. This location, after its sun house expansion is a good option to sit and do some work or chill and read books over Java. The location is difficult to park in yet for travelers leaving the Albrook airport it’s a better option than having coffee inside the airport.

As with any dining experience, staff is what makes it. My experience was very good with a staff, looking to please, and interested in providing a good cup.

I’d recommend they continue to offer well priced mug options like this one for those who don’t want to drink out of a paper cup.

You should leave with a bag of geisha, especially at the price that they offer.  Will visit again before departure from Albrook Airport.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-28T12:01:02.128686656Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "chetan chheda", "rating": 4, "text": "Kotowa is good Coffe and I also like their chocolate it's yummy", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-29T23:03:04.760621518Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Hilary Hughes", "rating": 5, "text": "They recently added another space, which is like a gazebo, super cute. I recommend downloading the kotowa app, it is very useful, gives you points, and a free beverage of your choice for your birthday.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-08-13T20:24:13.663779833Z", "relative_time": "10 months ago"}, {"author": "Joy Bessie W.", "rating": 5, "text": "I have been to this place since it first opened here in Albrook. Our little one got a Happy Meal with fries, juice, a burger/nuggets, AND the best surprise — a headset & visor toy to role-play as a McDonald's employee. 💛🎧
She took her "job" very seriously... while munching on fries on the side 😂

✔️ Tasty food
✔️ Fun toy
✔️ 10/10 cuteness

On the bright side of this place, everything costs very cheap.  Literally everything costs a dollar and fifty cents.  The food is plainly ok and it is expected to be such, yet that the dishes are more like entrees instead of main ones.  I think the purpose of this place is more of a "let's have a drink and chat" rather than a place where you can have a family gathering.  On the bad side, I think the place is too loud and there's not much of a variety from drinks and food to order.  But to maintain the low cost of all, it makes sense to have a very short menu.

schedule: Monday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Tuesday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Wednesday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Thursday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Morteza Farasat", "rating": 5, "text": "Salvaje was highly recommended in one of the YouTube videos I watched before going to Panama City, and it sure did not disappoint:
Fantastic service, delicious food (sushi rolls, sashimi/nigiri, and steak salvaje were outstanding) with beatiful presentations,  excellent beverages, and amazing desserts (coconut tres leches was to die for), combined with live entertainment made for an unforgettable dining experience for us; easily one of the top 3 dining experiences of 2026 for us (and we live in Las Vegas and eat out often). I highly recommend trying this restaurant. We will definitely go back when in Panama City again.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-05-26T21:19:31.230713482Z", "relative_time": "2 weeks ago"}, {"author": "Davia Gray", "rating": 5, "text": "We made our reservations beforehand and we got there on time. Our waiter Michael was AWESOME. He made recommendations and checked in very often. We ordered steak and ribs with potatoes. The food and drink portions were a VERY GOOD amount. The food was SO GOOD! I loved the ambiance and the entertainment. I definitely would recommend visiting anytime you’re in Panama!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-20T20:31:34.352447577Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Kat", "rating": 5, "text": "This was the best food I had in Panamá. Everything was exquisite. The drinks were spot on. Service was excellent. The only concern for me( because I don’t love a loud environment) is that it was VERYYYY loud. The reviews did say that but I underestimated it. I would probably return during the afternoon or early evening which would probably be quieter. The entertainment was very elevated as you can see in the video. One of the more expensive places we visited but still had affordable options on the menu- and so that felt inclusive. Loved seeing into the kitchen", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-30T17:00:24.814070802Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Todd Underwood", "rating": 5, "text": "The food and service was excellent!  We ordered several items to share. The Fuji Roll was super. One of the best rolls we have had in a long time. Why haven't we seen "bang bang" shrimp on top of a roll before. The Lobster dumplings were delicious. Just don't cover the dumpling in the sauce. The Short Rib Bao Buns were delicious. There are two on an order. We also ordered the Tempura Shishito Peppers. There are plenty of peppers to share for a group of 4. Note this may show as a roof top bar, but that was their old location. The new location is not a roof top bar.

¡La comida y servicio fueron excelentes! Pedimos varios platos para compartir. El fuji roll era super. Los lobster dumplings eran delicioso, pero no gustamos la salsa tanto. Los Short Rib Bao buns eran delicioso y tambien pedimos los Shishito peppers.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-23T21:35:14.565827413Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Mignon Brown", "rating": 5, "text": "SMH at the rage baiters on IG & Tik Tok who gave this restaurant 1 star! That was not our group’s experience, as those I tend to hang with don’t look for the party, they ARE the party!  Salvaje was a whole vibe. Music was in point & kept us dancing in our seats while dining and out of our seats for the birthday shout outs & shots! Food was delicious & masterfully prepared via Robata (grill), with a special shout out for the steak dish since I got to finish cooking it table side…temperature and flavor can’t be beat! Other notables include the branzino, Brussel sprouts, and fried rice. What’s not to love about a place that will keep me fed & entertained?!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-02T22:38:56.886977597Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}]

name: Mai Mai
address: Panamá Design Center (PDC, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 5:00 – 11:30 PM | Tuesday: 5:00 – 11:30 PM | Wednesday: 5:00 – 11:30 PM | Thursday: 5:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 5:00 – 11:30 PM | Saturday: 5:00 – 11:30 PM | Sunday: Closed
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Christian Conti", "rating": 5, "text": "Local friends took me here for my last dinner in Panama City, and we had an amazing time.

The space is super vibey and cool without being pretentious.  Service was attentive and well paced, seating was comfortable and fun.

We split a ton of stuff.  Ceviche, fried fish, tuna maki, lomo saltado, gyoza.  Everything was super tasty and well presented.  Cocktail program had a lot of interesting options, I ended up with a twist on a dirty martini. I was really fun and delicious.

Even got to meet the co-owners who were super friendly and gave us great recommendations for places to eat and drink on the rest of our trip, highly recommended!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-13T19:45:45.572756764Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Shark Hab", "rating": 5, "text": "This place offers truly delightful sushi. It’s impossible to imagine eating just a little when every dish bursts with flavor. I didn’t have a reservation, but they seated me at the counter, and honestly, that turned into a show of its own — watching the precise preparation of each roll was spectacular.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-30T22:15:36.607389727Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Jason park", "rating": 5, "text": "You don’t expect to find transcendent sushi in Panama. You expect heat, humidity, maybe a good rum, maybe a fish that still smells faintly of the sea. But Mai Mai flips that expectation on its head.
Tucked away in the chaos and charm of Panama, this place quietly delivers what most high-end spots only promise — authenticity, precision, and heart. The sushi was clean, balanced, almost reverent in its simplicity. The ceviche? A lightning bolt of freshness — sharp, bright, unapologetically alive.

Every bite reminded me why I travel. To be surprised. To be humbled by craftsmanship in the most unexpected corners of the world. Mai Mai isn’t trying to be Tokyo or Lima. It’s Panama — confident, generous, and wildly good.
The service matched the food — effortless, human, and deeply proud of what they serve. They even forgave my poor Spanish and worked hard to explain everything to me in English!

It was, without question, the capstone of my trip to see the Canal — a reminder that the world still has places where food isn’t just eaten, but experienced.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-21T15:24:57.943248121Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "Melissa Doucette", "rating": 5, "text": "You'll know a restaurant is well run when the food comes promptly, service is kind and friendly and the entire staff seems to be chill and enjoying their shift.

It was truly a joy to dine at maimai. The food was exceptional (ceviche nitro was the best ceviche I've ever had), we had fun watching the kitchen work and the staff interact with each other. The restaurant was busy but no one seemed stressed or upset, everything ran smooth from start to finish.

One of the coolest restaurants I've ever been in. I hope to be back someday.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-08T03:41:37.037233405Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Tiffany Choice", "rating": 5, "text": "Let me start by saying I love fine dining experiences and this is byfar one of the best, not just in Panamá but in many places I have traveled globally.  Although the evening started with a hiccup, everything was immediately addressed and remedied to end up with a beautiful dining experience at one of the best fine restaurants in Panama.  The ambiance and decor set the mood for a luxury dining experience at Mai Mai.  While difficult to find for newcomers, It is not hard to access.  Located in the far left corner of the plaza, take the elevator in the back corner (not the main in the parking lot) to the top floor.  As soon as you step off, you'll enter the vestibule where the automatic wall/ door will slide open, revealing a beautiful, modernly designed establishment.  This is perfect for dates, business or a night out for a good time.  The menu is extensive with unique Asian influence and Panamanian fusion.  My favorite items on the menu were the Tom Yum noodles because they have the rich robust Thai flavors directly tossed into the noodles rather than drowned in broth, and the coconut octopus bun.  These are two items remixed in a way that surprise the palate in ways I would have never imagined. I recommend going.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-04-27T13:31:14.741875Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}]

But very important message for European,
in PANAMA there is no regulation about the sound. And unfortunately, in this place it is just unbearable. You can have 85 to 90 decibels until 8:15 pm then suddenly, it went to 95 with some peaks close to 100 db !!!
A friend gave me earplugs so I can finish to leave. We obviously could not hear each other anymore, and we were a group of 6. So we finished to eat and had our last drink outside.
Maybe if you come before 8 pm it is good.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-09-28T05:00:49.872574951Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}]

schedule: Monday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Lucas Ribeiro", "rating": 5, "text": "Great restaurant near Santa Maria offices. Great staff, knowledgeable about celiac disease and with good amount of gluten free options. We had the steak, escargot, grilled cheese (great) , salad , fries and pure (without gravy) and the menu also has GF pasta.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-23T17:15:07.336529128Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Travelosivi", "rating": 4, "text": "Benny’s is a traditional American steakhouse with a nice, elegant decor and good service.

My husband and I tried the steak with pepper sauce, brussel sprouts, onion soup, and the grilled prawns. Everything was pretty tasty but too salty for our taste. Lychee martini was good.

Overall, it’s pretty good but they have to lower salt", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-05-31T14:50:22.532769Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "M. H.", "rating": 4, "text": "An American Contemporary Bistro offering innovative versions of iconic classics like Escargots in Bechamel Sauce, Barbecue Ribs with a twist or the Chicken Cordon Blue. Service is very friendly and professional. The atmosphere is cozy and elegant. Highly recommended!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-06-18T05:29:48.660064747Z", "relative_time": "12 months ago"}, {"author": "Oliver Garcia", "rating": 5, "text": "My wife and I recently had dinner at Benny’s, a new Argentinian-inspired steakhouse in Santamaria, Panama, and it was a fantastic experience from start to finish. The menu is simple, yet everything is perfectly executed.

We ordered the premium ribeye steak, cooked to a perfect medium, and it was outstanding—tender, juicy, and truly melted in the mouth. The broccolini side was a great complement, but the real surprise was the Caesar salad appetizer. Finally, a steakhouse in Panama with an outstanding Caesar salad! The empanadas de carne were also delicious, with a flavorful filling and perfect crust.

For dessert, I expected the typical New York cheesecake, but it far exceeded expectations. The wild red berry sauce was a delightful touch that took it to the next level.

If you’re in Panama and craving a quality steak dinner, Benny’s is a must-visit. Highly recommended!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-11-08T06:02:58.251773Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Nicola Esposito", "rating": 5, "text": "Best American steakhouse in Panama. I suggest ordering everything to share. Only opens on weekdays.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-18T17:41:42.945741230Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}]

[{"author": "Linda Petiot", "rating": 5, "text": "We ordered the family brisket &ribs meal (for 2). The first thing my guest said when he had a bite of brisket was "That's what brisket is supposed to be like."

It was very good, and they give you a whole bottle of bbq sauce to dispense as you see fit.
The ribs were very good too. As were the sides of beans (could have used more) and the crispy onions.

Music was a mix of classic rock and country, which worked for both of us.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-13T18:04:54.083560351Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Christian Ortega", "rating": 5, "text": "Frank’s service was outstanding and made me wanna come back.
I recommend getting the 4x4 platter for 4 people, meat is great, sides are decent and could be better, would not recommend getting the Mac and cheese as they lack seasoning
The guard at the parking lot was rude. I went with a pregnant lady and he did not believe she was pregnant and told be I won’t be going to heaven because I was a liar.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-09-27T15:48:42.078569385Z", "relative_time": "8 months ago"}, {"author": "Vessel Of YAH", "rating": 1, "text": "Been coming here awhile but Wednesday's night experience almost cost me my life. The service was horrible and so was the food. The waitress knew we had a food allergy and has known this for months. Wednesday they decided to lie and ignore it. Costing me a severe allergic reaction and had to seek medical treatment. I reached out to the establishment and they read my email but never responded. Shows they could careless and no accountability. STAY AWAY AND BEWARE. MY EMAIL TO THEM BELOW:
Hello, we just left your establishment tonight and had a horrible experience. We have been coming here since September and expressed that we're allergic to pork. Tonight we ordered our regular 2 chillies no onions in one a 1 okalahoma burger plain well done and another one well done with everything on it. Side of curly fries and 2 balboas. Tonight we never got our burgers but got the chili. The chili was very oiling and must of had pork in it. Because of the cross contamination I'm now having a severe allergic reaction, sharp pains in my stomach, headaches and vomiting. If this continues will need to go to the hospital. Here are the photos attached. This is unsafe and unacceptable. You can kill someone cross contamination especially knowing the customers allergies.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-27T20:21:49.838622705Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Jorge Alegria", "rating": 5, "text": "First time at Austin Mama’s and I loved it !!! Great atmosphere, music, colorful place full of lots of details! When going up the stairs I really felt I was like “Beam me up Scotty!” from Panama to Texas! And still, Best of All! Karen, our server, who took great care of our party of 12 guests, great attention to all details, and a beautiful smile! Finally, food was, in one word, PERFECT!", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-05T04:31:45.080589010Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Dee", "rating": 5, "text": "We hit up the food truck in Coronado, it was delicious!! The Bruja was crisp and clear, the porter (I forget the name) was rich and complex. The snacks (dirty fries, chicken nibbles with spicy honey) were well portioned and tasty.  For the ignorant traveler like us - grab a table outside and use the QR code on the table to put your order in. You still have to pay a person tho, the app says it's charging you but it's not really doing that,  it's just for ordering.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-19T15:30:23.094193539Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Christina Castiglione", "rating": 5, "text": "Amazing brewery tucked away in an industrial park. The beer itself was delicious and the food was excellent! Great service from the bartenders as well. Recommend the Chivaperro beer and the brisket quesadilla.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-12-29T03:15:34.606142028Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}, {"author": "FrosT", "rating": 1, "text": "***UPDATE Sept 9 2003 (4 down to 1 ⭐)***

So it's 🌮  Taco Week  and their creation sounds pretty damn good (and it was... 4 out of 5 only because the pork was cold) so this is where we decided to go.

Ordered the taco week at 9:40pm and then another separate order of 2 glasses of water, 1 OJ, 1 starter and 2 main dishes at 9:47pm via their online/phone system.

Something like 5 mins went by and they brought the pager for the taco week. Another 10 minutes went by and one of the servers came to ask us if we wanted something to drink. I told him we had ordered 2 waters, OJ and a taco week (which brings a drink) but hadn't received anything yet. He said he would go check and get then right away and so he did.

The beeper went off 5 minutes later and I went to grab the food, thinking it would all be there (cause a total of 30+ minutes has gone by) but no, only the taco week was ready.

20 more minutes went by and another server came by and asked if we wanted anything else. I told him we were still waiting on food and he seemed surprised, said he would check and I told him at this point to forget about it if it hadn't been ordered (which it was, cause I ordered it via their system and received a confirmation #) 🤨.

He came back and said that in effect there was some sort of system error and that it would take another 30 minutes (it was 10:40pm at this point, an hour after we had arrived) if we wanted the rest of the food (which was my GFs basically). We declined and I paid everything  except the OJ which she didn't touch because her food never arrived.

The server did offer us a free beer or OJ but what he should have done was waive off the whole thing, horrible experience 👎🏻👎🏻 and I basically had to then go find another restaurant at almost 11PM for my GF to eat.

P.S. Don't make customers use a system to order that doesn't work.

***Old Review***

Wednesday Trivia Nights are fun, wish they had a bigger selection of alcoholic drinks for those who don't drink/like beer or the cider/hard seltzer options served here.

Pulled Pork Quesadillas are nice!

The French Fries with feta are quite spicy so be carefu when orderingl if someone doesn't like spicy food.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2023-09-10T04:50:30.457840Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}, {"author": "Pete Zivkov", "rating": 5, "text": "Casa Bruja is my favorite place for craft beer in Panama City. They offer an excellent variety of flavorful beer styles served in a comfortable brewery tasting room. We also enjoyed the She Burger and Chicken Strips.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2022-03-06T01:01:35.010640Z", "relative_time": "4 years ago"}]

Pros:

- The service and ambience were fine, orders were taken quickly, drinks served quickly and the bill was fast too

- Toilets were nice

- I had a solid martini

- Bread was excellent

- Squid was exceptionally good

Cons:

- We had the seafood platter and I was generally slightly disappointed. The entire thing was overly drenched in oil and garlic, which took away some of the taste

name: La Rana Dorada
address: Vía Argentina 20, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
types: brewpub, pub, bar, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment
schedule: Monday: 12:00 PM – 12:29 AM | Tuesday: 12:00 PM – 12:30 AM | Wednesday: 12:00 PM – 12:30 AM | Thursday: 12:00 PM – 1:30 AM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 3:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 3:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 PM – 12:30 AM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Rudy GC", "rating": 3, "text": "La Rana Dorada is one of those places in Panama City that instantly grabs your attention with its lively atmosphere and well-known reputation for craft beer. On that front, they truly deliver. The beer is excellent—fresh, flavorful, and clearly made with care. Whether you prefer something light and crisp or a more robust brew, their selection offers a satisfying variety. It’s definitely one of the highlights of the experience and the main reason I would consider returning.

The food, overall, is good. The menu has a solid mix of pub-style dishes and more elaborate options, and everything we tried tasted well-prepared and enjoyable. Portions were reasonable and the presentation was appealing. However, despite the quality, the experience was overshadowed by how long it took to receive our order. What should have been a relaxed meal ended up feeling dragged out because the kitchen was extremely slow that day. Waiting that long definitely affected the rhythm of the visit.

The service itself was friendly, but the delays made it hard to fully enjoy the time there. For that reason, I would rate La Rana Dorada three stars. Great beer, good food, but the slow delivery of our order kept the experience from being as good as it could have been. With faster service, this place could easily reach four or even five stars.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-16T05:28:48.573311621Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "Tarun Yadav", "rating": 5, "text": "Nice brewpub in El Cangrejo, about a minute from Einstein's Head statue. Have both indoor and outside seating, where you can watch the world go by. Their IPA is the best beer they have. Had a snack too - nachos were decent . RECOMMEND.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-10-23T10:44:43.407206216Z", "relative_time": "7 months ago"}, {"author": "Mark Davis", "rating": 5, "text": "La Ronda Dorada was very cool. Reminiscent of a British pub and has a great feel with lots of expats hanging around there. We enjoyed the experience. The Hamburguesa was solid and the rest of the food was pretty good. Overall, I recommend it. Check out the one in Casco Viejo for a larger and more historic looking place. That location seemed newer but had a vibe that was appropriate to the old town.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-27T00:16:24.889302847Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Manny Vargas", "rating": 5, "text": "The perfect way to start your Panama adventure. Good food, good beer and right on the sidewalk. Love it.

I got the bacon burger. When it was brought, I thought I made need another. I actually had to force the last few bites. It was perfect 🤩

The beer was smooth and delicious.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-10T23:54:47.975255509Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Ariel Felipe Torres", "rating": 5, "text": "I’m going to write this review because I saw something worth highlighting.
The Rana beers have always been very good and the pizza always delivers with great flavor, but that particular day I sat at the bar and there was a guy who seemed to be on his first day. The other bartender was teaching him with a great attitude, and the new guy kept asking questions to make sure he was doing things right, and the bartender always responded in a very kind and helpful way.

That is the foundation of good customer service: if there isn’t a good work environment, the service usually ends up being terrible. But here I saw how well they treat each other as coworkers, and that shows when they attend to customers.

Great attitude, keep it up.
I’ll be coming back more often.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-08-19T21:52:39.183137463Z", "relative_time": "9 months ago"}]

[{"author": "Lanae Moreno", "rating": 5, "text": "Absolutely loved this spot in Panama! 🇵🇦
I had breakfast at Sucaro Cafe Bakery and Piandinas and everything was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The waffle plate with eggs, avocado, ham, and cheese was delicious and perfectly balanced, and the maracuyá juice was incredibly refreshing and full of natural flavor.

The atmosphere was calm, cozy, and welcoming — the perfect place to relax and enjoy a great meal while visiting Panama. You can really tell they care about quality and presentation. reviews_json:
[{"author": "Daniel George", "rating": 5, "text": "We really enjoyed Fonda Mamá. When you go in, you pass through an area where the food is displayed. The servers dish up the food and answer any questions you have about the items. They have a soda machine for drinks, and bottled water. Then you pay at the register and pick a table.
We liked the food so much that my wife bought another helping while I shoppped at the next-door produce store.
The extra snack made it back on the short walk to our hotel, but not much longer.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-02-12T15:59:12.027479516Z", "relative_time": "4 months ago"}, {"author": "Joe Mari Francisco", "rating": 5, "text": "Fonda Mama is one of the must-try local food establishments in Panama. The staffs don't speak English but they are very helpful. The food is great, especially the chorizos and fresh juices.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-05-31T15:32:16.744794Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}, {"author": "Zeid Tisnes", "rating": 5, "text": "DISCLAIMER: this is not a restaurant with luxuries servers or whatever. This is a typical Panamanian food and excellent savory of what Panama is.

One of my favorite go-to vegan spots if I want to save some cash. The portions are relatively good. I order a plate of rice, lentils/beans, and sometimes "tajadas". Once paid, choose a table (if eating inside) and find one of the spicy Caribbean sauces. Is not super spicy, but it does add a good umami taste and kick.

A plate of rice + lentils is 2.25$ (or 2.75$ I don't remember). With the tajada, is like an extra 1.50$ for 2 tajadas (normal portion)", "language": "en", "published_at": "2025-11-17T03:26:28.425124702Z", "relative_time": "6 months ago"}, {"author": "Eleonora", "rating": 5, "text": "We were looking for a place frequented by locals in Panama, and Mamá Gallina definitely delivered. The ladies at the counter were very patient with us as newbies to Panamanian cuisine and helped us choose what to order. Even a lady who was in line after me helped us! The food was good, and the portions were abundant (by our European standards). Prices are very affordable compared to other places in Panama. For example, I ordered a full meal (see photo) for about $8. Credit card payment is available. There is no air conditioning, but fans were enough to keep us cool enough on a very hot day (34°C).", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-04-01T11:44:22.116918Z", "relative_time": "2 years ago"}, {"author": "Marion Fauchon Digital Marketing Freelance", "rating": 5, "text": "We loved this place! So cheap, delicious and authentic. Our Spanish wasn’t great but the locals helped us translate and everyone was super friendly. We would definitely come back and highly recommend this place for an authentic experience.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2024-09-19T23:28:01.570116Z", "relative_time": "a year ago"}]

name: El Caribe Restaurant
address: Vía Argentina 12, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama
schedule: Monday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM
reviews_json:
[{"author": "Tina Green", "rating": 5, "text": "I recently visited El Caribe Restaurant and had a wonderful experience from start to finish. I was greeted by a young man who warmly welcomed me and allowed me to choose my seating. They were very busy, so there was a short wait for service, but it was completely understandable given how popular the restaurant clearly is.
I ordered the ginger pineapple drink — absolutely delicious and refreshing. For my meal, I had the salmon with house sauce, rice & peas, and sweet plantains. Everything came out hot, flavorful, and perfectly seasoned. The food was fresh and truly satisfying.
The servers were attentive, personable, and made sure I had everything I needed throughout my visit. I also had the pleasure of meeting the owner, Maritza Ellis, which made the experience feel even more special and personal.
Overall, 10/10 — I highly recommend this spot if you’re looking for amazing Caribbean flavors and welcoming service in Panama City.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-01T23:29:55.707650425Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Team Breakdown", "rating": 5, "text": "Jamaican vibe. Food was amazing. The owner told us that if we wasn’t 100% satisfied, the meal was on her.

I had the red snapper with brown stew, gravy, and sweet plantains. Taste like I was back in Fort Lauderdale.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-08T07:24:28.504635528Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Erica Honey", "rating": 5, "text": "Delicious, authentic food.   Ordered 2 oxtail plates and 2 natural juices.  We will definitely be back in a couple of days.  Samantha, our server was a delight.  5 🌟

Edited to add: 2nd day in a row.  We ordered the brown stew chicken and brown stew snapper.   Sooooo tasty and flavorful.   Met the owner, Maritza and she was welcoming and you can tell she takes pride in her restaurant.   Pics added thos time around because the 1st visit, we demolished our plates before realizing.  Lpl", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-03-07T19:55:58.701123937Z", "relative_time": "3 months ago"}, {"author": "Robin Burnett-Parker", "rating": 4, "text": "First time in Panama and this meal was delicious 😋.
Oxtail are a must. Blackened fish was amazing.
Friendly staff and atmosphere was great as well as other customers were extremely friendly too.", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-06-06T15:44:05.865910623Z", "relative_time": "a week ago"}, {"author": "Marouna Gomes", "rating": 5, "text": "My cousin and I came here at the end of August 2025 and we had the best experience that we came back the next night. The food is incredible - we got oxtail and dumplings and the fish. The service is super friendly and kind, the owner came over to say hey to us and our waitress was super attentive. The place is cute and warm with good music playing. I think about this place all the time.. love from Canada ", "language": "en", "published_at": "2026-01-15T23:27:29.338205917Z", "relative_time": "5 months ago"}]
